#include \\\"test/mocks/ssl/mocks.h\\\"\\n19: #include \\\"test/mocks/upstream/load_balancer.h\\\"\\n20: #include \\\"test/mocks/upstream/load_balancer_context.h\\\"\\n21: #include \\\"test/mocks/upstream/priority_set.h\\\"\\n22: #include \\\"test/test_common/environment.h\\\"\\n23: #include \\\"test/test_common/test_runtime.h\\\"\\n24: \\n25: using testing::AtLeast;\\n26: using testing::DoAll;\\n27: using testing::InSequence;\\n28: using testing::Return;\\n29: using testing::SizeIs;\\n30: \\n31: namespace Envoy {\\n32: namespace Extensions {\\n33: namespace Clusters {\\n34: namespace DynamicForwardProxy {\\n35: \\n36: class ClusterTest : public testing::Test,\\n37:                     public Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCacheManagerFactory {\\n38: public:\\n39:   void initialize(const std::string& yaml_config, bool uses_tls) {\\n40:     envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster cluster_config =\\n41:         Upstream::parseClusterFromV3Yaml(yaml_config);\\n42:     envoy::extensions::clusters::dynamic_forward_proxy::v3::ClusterConfig config;\\n43:     Config::Utility::translateOpaqueConfig(cluster_config.cluster_type().typed_config(),\\n44:                                            ProtobufMessage::getStrictValidationVisitor(), config);\\n45: \\n46:     Envoy::Upstream::ClusterFactoryContextImpl factory_context(\\n47:         server_context_, server_context_.cluster_manager_, nullptr, ssl_context_manager_, nullptr,\\n48:         false);\\n49: \\n50:     ON_CALL(server_context_, api()).WillByDefault(testing::ReturnRef(*api_));\\n51: \\n52:     if (uses_tls) {\\n53:       EXPECT_CALL(ssl_context_manager_, createSslClientContext(_, _));\\n54:     }\\n55:     EXPECT_CALL(*dns_cache_manager_, getCache(_));\\n56:     // Below we return a nullptr handle which has no effect on the code under test but isn't\\n57:     // actually correct. It's possible this will have to change in the future.\\n58:     EXPECT_CALL(*dns_cache_manager_->dns_cache_, addUpdateCallbacks_(_))\\n59:         .WillOnce(DoAll(SaveArgAddress(&update_callbacks_), Return(nullptr)));\\n60:     auto cache = dns_cache_manager_->getCache(config.dns_cache_config()).value();\\n61:     cluster_.reset(\\n62:         new Cluster(cluster_config, std::move(cache), config, factory_context, this->get()));\\n63:     thread_aware_lb_ = std::make_unique<Cluster::ThreadAwareLoadBalancer>(*cluster_);\\n64:     lb_factory_ = thread_aware_lb_->factory();\\n65:     refreshLb();\\n66: \\n67:     ON_CALL(lb_context_, downstreamHeaders()).WillByDefault(Return(&downstream_headers_));\\n68:     ON_CALL(connection_, streamInfo()).WillByDefault(ReturnRef(stream_info_));\\n69:     ON_CALL(lb_context_, requestStreamInfo()).WillByDefault(Return(&stream_info_));\\n70:     ON_CALL(lb_context_, downstreamConnection()).WillByDefault(Return(&connection_));\\n71: \\n72:     member_update_cb_ = cluster_->prioritySet().addMemberUpdateCb(\\n73:         [this](const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_added,\\n74:                const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_removed) -> void {\\n75:           onMemberUpdateCb(hosts_added, hosts_removed);\\n76:         });\\n77: \\n78:     absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr>\\n79:         existing_hosts;\\n80:     for (const auto& host : host_map_) {\\n81:       existing_hosts.emplace(host.first, host.second);\\n82:     }\\n83:     EXPECT_CALL(*dns_cache_manager_->dns_cache_, iterateHostMap(_)).WillOnce(Invoke([&](auto cb) {\\n84:       for (const auto& host : host_map_) {\\n85:         cb(host.first, host.second);\\n86:       }\\n87:     }));\\n88:     if (!existing_hosts.empty()) {\\n89:       EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(existing_hosts.size()), SizeIs(0)));\\n90:     }\\n91:     cluster_->initialize([] {});\\n92:   }\\n93: \\n94:   Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCacheManagerSharedPtr get() override {\\n95:     return dns_cache_manager_;\\n96:   }\\n97: \\n98:   void makeTestHost(const std::string& host, const std::string& address) {\\n99:     EXPECT_TRUE(host_map_.find(host) == host_map_.end());\\n100:     host_map_[host] = std::make_shared<Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::MockDnsHostInfo>();\\n101:     host_map_[host]->address_ = Network::Utility::parseInternetAddress(address);\\n102: \\n103:     // Allow touch() to still be strict.\\n104:     EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], address()).Times(AtLeast(0));\\n105:     EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], addressList()).Times(AtLeast(0));\\n106:     EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], isIpAddress()).Times(AtLeast(0));\\n107:     EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], resolvedHost()).Times(AtLeast(0));\\n108:   }\\n109: \\n110:   void updateTestHostAddress(const std::string& host, const std::string& address) {\\n111:     EXPECT_FALSE(host_map_.find(host) == host_map_.end());\\n112:     host_map_[host]->address_ = Network::Utility::parseInternetAddress(address);\\n113:   }\\n114: \\n115:   void refreshLb() { lb_ = lb_factory_->create(lb_params_); }\\n116: \\n117:   Upstream::MockLoadBalancerContext* setHostAndReturnContext(const std::string& host) {\\n118:     downstream_headers_.remove(\\\":authority\\\");\\n119:     downstream_headers_.addCopy(\\\":authority\\\", host);\\n120:     return &lb_context_;\\n121:   }\\n122: \\n123:   Upstream::MockLoadBalancerContext* setFilterStateHostAndReturnContext(const std::string& host) {\\n124:     StreamInfo::FilterState& filter_state =\\n125:         const_cast<StreamInfo::FilterState&>(lb_context_.requestStreamInfo()->filterState());\\n126: \\n127:     filter_state.setData(\\n128:         \\\"envoy.upstream.dynamic_host\\\", std::make_shared<Router::StringAccessorImpl>(host),\\n129:         StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable, StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Connection,\\n130:         StreamInfo::StreamSharingMayImpactPooling::SharedWithUpstreamConnection);\\n131: \\n132:     return &lb_context_;\\n133:   }\\n134: \\n135:   void setOutlierFailed(const std::string& host) {\\n136:     for (auto& h : cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()) {\\n137:       if (h->hostname() == host) {\\n138:         h->healthFlagSet(Upstream::Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_OUTLIER_CHECK);\\n139:         break;\\n140:       }\\n141:     }\\n142:   }\\n143: \\n144:   void clearOutlierFailed(const std::string& host) {\\n145:     for (auto& h : cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()) {\\n146:       if (h->hostname() == host) {\\n147:         h->healthFlagClear(Upstream::Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_OUTLIER_CHECK);\\n148:         break;\\n149:       }\\n150:     }\\n151:   }\\n152: \\n153:   MOCK_METHOD(void, onMemberUpdateCb,\\n154:               (const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_added, const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_removed));\\n155: \\n156:   NiceMock<Server::Configuration::MockServerFactoryContext> server_context_;\\n157:   Stats::TestUtil::TestStore& stats_store_ = server_context_.store_;\\n158:   Api::ApiPtr api_{Api::createApiForTest(stats_store_)};\\n159:   Ssl::MockContextManager ssl_context_manager_;\\n160: \\n161:   std::shared_ptr<Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::MockDnsCacheManager> dns_cache_manager_{\\n162:       new Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::MockDnsCacheManager()};\\n163:   std::shared_ptr<Cluster> cluster_;\\n164:   Upstream::ThreadAwareLoadBalancerPtr thread_aware_lb_;\\n165:   Upstream::LoadBalancerFactorySharedPtr lb_factory_;\\n166:   Upstream::LoadBalancerPtr lb_;\\n167:   NiceMock<Upstream::MockLoadBalancerContext> lb_context_;\\n168:   Http::TestRequestHeaderMapImpl downstream_headers_;\\n169:   Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCache::UpdateCallbacks* update_callbacks_{};\\n170:   absl::flat_hash_map<std::string,\\n171:                       std::shared_ptr<Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::MockDnsHostInfo>>\\n172:       host_map_;\\n173:   Envoy::Common::CallbackHandlePtr member_update_cb_;\\n174:   NiceMock<StreamInfo::MockStreamInfo> stream_info_;\\n175:   NiceMock<Network::MockConnection> connection_;\\n176: \\n177:   // Just use this as parameters of create() method but thread aware load balancer will not use it.\\n178:   NiceMock<Upstream::MockPrioritySet> worker_priority_set_;\\n179:   Upstream::LoadBalancerParams lb_params_{worker_priority_set_, {}};\\n180: \\n181:   const std::string sub_cluster_yaml_config_ = R\\\"EOF(\\n182: name: name\\n183: connect_timeout: 0.25s\\n184: cluster_type:\\n185:   name: dynamic_forward_proxy\\n186:   typed_config:\\n187:     \\\"@type\\\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\\n188:     sub_clusters_config:\\n189:       max_sub_clusters: 1024\\n190: )EOF\\\";\\n191: \\n192:   const std::string default_yaml_config_ = R\\\"EOF(\\n193: name: name\\n194: connect_timeout: 0.25s\\n195: cluster_type:\\n196:   name: dynamic_forward_proxy\\n197:   typed_config:\\n198:     \\\"@type\\\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\\n199:     dns_cache_config:\\n200:       name: foo\\n201:       dns_lookup_family: AUTO\\n202: )EOF\\\";\\n203: \\n204:   const std::string coalesce_connection_config_ = R\\\"EOF(\\n205: name: name\\n206: connect_timeout: 0.25s\\n207: cluster_type:\\n208:   name: dynamic_forward_proxy\\n209:   typed_config:\\n210:     \\\"@type\\\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\\n211:     allow_coalesced_connections: true\\n212:     dns_cache_config:\\n213:       name: foo\\n214:       dns_lookup_family: AUTO\\n215: )EOF\\\";\\n216: };\\n217: \\n218: // createSubClusterConfig twice.\\n219: TEST_F(ClusterTest, CreateSubClusterConfig) {\\n220:   initialize(sub_cluster_yaml_config_, false);\\n221: \\n222:   const std::string cluster_name = \\\"fake_cluster_name\\\";\\n223:   const std::string host = \\\"localhost\\\";\\n224:   const int port = 80;\\n225:   std::pair<bool, absl::optional<envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster>> sub_cluster_pair =\\n226:       cluster_->createSubClusterConfig(cluster_name, host, port);\\n227:   EXPECT_EQ(true, sub_cluster_pair.first);\\n228:   EXPECT_EQ(true, sub_cluster_pair.second.has_value());\\n229: \\n230:   // create again, already exists\\n231:   sub_cluster_pair = cluster_->createSubClusterConfig(cluster_name, host, port);\\n232:   EXPECT_EQ(true, sub_cluster_pair.first);\\n233:   EXPECT_EQ(false, sub_cluster_pair.second.has_value());\\n234: }\\n235: \\n236: // Basic flow of the cluster including adding hosts and removing them.\\n237: TEST_F(ClusterTest, BasicFlow) {\\n238:   initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\\n239:   makeTestHost(\\\"host1:0\\\", \\\"1.2.3.4\\\");\\n240:   InSequence s;\\n241: \\n242:   EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"\\\")));\\n243: \\n244:   // Verify no host LB cases.\\n245:   EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"foo\\\")));\\n246:   EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->peekAnotherHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"foo\\\")));\\n247: \\n248:   // LB will immediately resolve host1.\\n249:   EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\\n250:   update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\\\"host1:0\\\", host_map_[\\\"host1:0\\\"]);\\n251:   EXPECT_EQ(1UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\\n252:   EXPECT_EQ(\\\"1.2.3.4:0\\\",\\n253:             cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()[0]->address()->asString());\\n254:   EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\\\"host1:0\\\"], touch());\\n255:   EXPECT_EQ(\\\"1.2.3.4:0\\\",\\n256:             lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"host1:0\\\"))->address()->asString());\\n257: \\n258:   // After changing the address, LB will immediately resolve the new address with a refresh.\\n259:   updateTestHostAddress(\\\"host1:0\\\", \\\"2.3.4.5\\\");\\n260:   update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\\\"host1:0\\\", host_map_[\\\"host1:0\\\"]);\\n261:   EXPECT_EQ(1UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\\n262:   EXPECT_EQ(\\\"2.3.4.5:0\\\",\\n263:             cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()[0]->address()->asString());\\n264:   EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\\\"host1:0\\\"], touch());\\n265:   EXPECT_EQ(\\\"2.3.4.5:0\\\",\\n266:             lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"host1:0\\\"))->address()->asString());\\n267: \\n268:   // Remove the host, LB will immediately fail to find the host in the map.\\n269:   EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(0), SizeIs(1)));\\n270:   update_callbacks_->onDnsHostRemove(\\\"host1:0\\\");\\n271:   EXPECT_EQ(0UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\\n272:   EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"host1:0\\\")));\\n273: }\\n274: \\n275: // Outlier detection\\n276: TEST_F(ClusterTest, OutlierDetection) {\\n277:   initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\\n278:   makeTestHost(\\\"host1:0\\\", \\\"1.2.3.4\\\");\\n279:   makeTestHost(\\\"host2:0\\\", \\\"5.6.7.8\\\");\\n280:   InSequence s;\\n281: \\n282:   EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\\n283:   update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\\\"host1:0\\\", host_map_[\\\"host1:0\\\"]);\\n284:   EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\\\"host1:0\\\"], touch());\\n285:   EXPECT_EQ(\\\"1.2.3.4:0\\\",\\n286:             lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"host1:0\\\"))->address()->asString());\\n287: \\n288:   EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\\n289:   update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\\\"host2:0\\\", host_map_[\\\"host2:0\\\"]);\\n290:   EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\\\"host2:0\\\"], touch());\\n291:   EXPECT_EQ(\\\"5.6.7.8:0\\\",\\n292:             lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"host2:0\\\"))->address()->asString());\\n293: \\n294:   // Fail outlier check for host1\\n295:   setOutlierFailed(\\\"host1:0\\\");\\n296:   EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"host1:0\\\")));\\n297:   // \\\"host2:0\\\" should not be affected\\n298:   EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\\\"host2:0\\\"], touch());\\n299:   EXPECT_EQ(\\\"5.6.7.8:0\\\",\\n300:             lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"host2:0\\\"))->address()->asString());\\n301: \\n302:   // Clear outlier check failure for host1, it should be available again\\n303:   clearOutlierFailed(\\\"host1:0\\\");\\n304:   EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\\\"host1:0\\\"], touch());\\n305:   EXPECT_EQ(\\\"1.2.3.4:0\\\",\\n306:             lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\\\"host1:0\\\"))->address()->asString());\\n307: }\\n308: \\n309: // Various invalid LB context permutations in case the cluster is used outside of HTTP.\\n310: TEST_F(ClusterTest, InvalidLbContext) {\\n311:   initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\\n312:   ON_CALL(lb_context_, downstreamHeaders()).WillByDefault(Return(nullptr));\\n313:   EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(&lb_context_));\\n314:   EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(nullptr));\\n315: }\\n316: \\n317: TEST_F(ClusterTest, FilterStateHostOverride) {\\n318:   initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\\n319:   makeTestHost(\\\"host1:0\\\", \\\"1.2.3.4\\\");\\n320: \\n321:   EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\\n322:   update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\\\"host1:0\\\", host_map_[\\\"host1:0\\\"]);\\n323:   EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\\\"host1:0\\\"], touch());\\n324:   EXPECT_EQ(\\\"1.2.3.4:0\\\",\\n325:             lb_->chooseHost(setFilterStateHostAndReturnContext(\\\"host1:0\\\"))->address()->asString());\\n326: }\\n327: \\n328: // Verify cluster attaches to a populated cache.\\n329: TEST_F(ClusterTest, PopulatedCache) {\\n330:   makeTestHost(\\\"host1:0\\\", \\\"1.2.3.4\\\");\\n331:   makeTestHost(\\\"host2:0\\\", \\\"1.2.3.5\\\");\\n332:   initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\\n333:   EXPECT_EQ(2UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\\n334: }\\n335: \\n336: TEST_F(ClusterTest, LoadBalancer_LifetimeCallbacksWithoutCoalescing) {\\n337:   initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\\n338: \\n339:   OptRef<Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::ConnectionLifetimeCallbacks> lifetime_callbacks =\\n340:       lb_->lifetimeCallbacks();\\n341:   ASSERT_FALSE(lifetime_callbacks.has_value());\\n342: }\\n343: \\n344: TEST_F(ClusterTest, LoadBalancer_LifetimeCallbacksWithCoalescing) {\\n345:   initialize(coalesce_connection_config_, false);\\n346: \\n347:   OptRef<Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::ConnectionLifetimeCallbacks> lifetime_callbacks =\\n348:       lb_->lifetimeCallbacks();\\n349:   ASSERT_TRUE(lifetime_callbacks.has_value());\\n350: }\\n351: \\n352: TEST_F(ClusterTest, LoadBalancer_SelectPoolNoConnections) {\\n353:   initialize(coalesce_connection_config_, false);\\n354: \\n355:   const std::string hostname = \\\"mail.example.org\\\";\\n356:   Upstream::MockHost host;\\n357:   EXPECT_CALL(host, hostname()).WillRepeatedly(testing::ReturnRef(hostname));\\n358:   Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address =\\n359:       Network::Utility::resolveUrl(\\\"tcp://10.0.0.3:50000\\\");\\n360:   EXPECT_CALL(host, address()).WillRepeatedly(testing::Return(address));\\n361:   std::vector<uint8_t> hash_key = {1, 2, 3};\\n362: \\n363:   absl::optional<Upstream::SelectedPoolAndConnection> selection =\\n364:       lb_->selectExistingConnection(&lb_context_, host, hash_key);\\n365: \\n366:   EXPECT_FALSE(selection.has_value());\\n367: }\\n368: \\n369: TEST_F(ClusterTest, LoadBalancer_SelectPoolMatchingConnection) {\\n370:   initialize(coalesce_connection_config_, false);\\n371: \\n372:   const std::string hostname = \\\"mail.example.org\\\";\\n373:   Upstream::MockHost host;\\n374:   EXPECT_CALL(host, hostname()).WillRepeatedly(testing::ReturnRef(hostname));\\n375:   Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address =\\n376:       Network::Utility::resolveUrl(\\\"tcp://10.0.0.3:50000\\\");\\n377:   EXPECT_CALL(host, address()).WillRepeatedly(testing::Return(address));\\n378:   std::vector<uint8_t> hash_key = {1, 2, 3};\\n379: auto_san_validation true unless \\\"\\n692:       \\\"allow_insecure_cluster_options is set.\\\");\\n693: }\\n694: \\n695: TEST_F(ClusterFactoryTest, InsecureUpstreamHttpProtocolOptions) {\\n696: 10. think @ 2026-03-10T19:26:11.648045

Input

{
  "thought": "Now I have a clear picture of the code. Let me analyze what needs to be changed:\n\n1. **upstream_impl.cc**: Add debug logging to `updateCrossPriorityHostMap` for cross-priority host map mutations (insert and erase operations).\n\n2. **cluster.cc**: Refactor `addOrUpdateHost()` to:\n   - Instead of updating the address in-place with `setNewAddresses()`, remove the old host and create a new `LogicalHost`\n   - Call `updatePriorityState()` directly within `addOrUpdateHost()` (both for new hosts and updated hosts)\n   - Remove the `hosts_added` out-parameter pattern\n\n3. **cluster.h**: Simplify the `addOrUpdateHost()` signature to remove the `hosts_added` out-parameter.\n\n4. **cluster_test.cc**: Update test expectations for the new per-host update notification pattern. Looking at the `BasicFlow` test:\n   - Currently, when address changes, there's no `onMemberUpdateCb` call expected\n   - With the new approach (remove old + add new), we'd expect `onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(1))` for address updates\n\nLet me look at the test more carefully:\n\n```cpp\n// After changing the address, LB will immediately resolve the new address with a refresh.\nupdateTestHostAddress(\"host1:0\", \"2.3.4.5\");\nupdate_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\nEXPECT_EQ(1UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\nEXPECT_EQ(\"2.3.4.5:0\",\n          cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()[0]->address()->asString());\nEXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\nEXPECT_EQ(\"2.3.4.5:0\",\n          lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n```\n\nWith the new approach, when address changes, we'd expect `onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(1))` to be called. The test currently doesn't have this expectation.\n\nNow let me think about the implementation:\n\n### cluster.h changes:\n- Change `addOrUpdateHost` signature from:\n  ```cpp\n  void addOrUpdateHost(absl::string_view host,\n                       const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info,\n                       std::unique_ptr<Upstream::HostVector>& hosts_added)\n      ABSL_LOCKS_EXCLUDED(host_map_lock_);\n  ```\n  to:\n  ```cpp\n  void addOrUpdateHost(absl::string_view host,\n                       const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info)\n      ABSL_LOCKS_EXCLUDED(host_map_lock_);\n  ```\n\n### cluster.cc changes:\n- `startPreInit()`: Remove the `hosts_added` pattern, call `addOrUpdateHost` directly, and collect hosts for `updatePriorityState` differently. Actually, looking at the description more carefully:\n  - \"The method signature is simplified to remove the accumulated `hosts_added` vector pattern\"\n  - \"updatePriorityState() is called directly within addOrUpdateHost()\"\n  \n  So `addOrUpdateHost()` will call `updatePriorityState()` itself.\n  \n  But wait, `startPreInit()` iterates over all hosts and calls `addOrUpdateHost` for each. If `addOrUpdateHost` calls `updatePriorityState` for each host, that would be inefficient. Let me re-read the description...\n\n  \"The fix refactors `addOrUpdateHost()` to remove the old host and create a new `LogicalHost` with the new address, properly emitting both `hosts_added` and `hosts_removed` events to the priority set.\"\n  \n  \"The method signature is simplified to remove the accumulated `hosts_added` vector pattern, and `updatePriorityState()` is called directly within `addOrUpdateHost()`.\"\n\n  So the new `addOrUpdateHost()` will:\n  1. If host exists with different address: remove old host from host_map_, create new LogicalHost, add to host_map_, call updatePriorityState({new_host}, {old_host})\n  2. If host doesn't exist: create new LogicalHost, add to host_map_, call updatePriorityState({new_host}, {})\n\n  And `startPreInit()` will just call `addOrUpdateHost` for each host without collecting hosts_added.\n\n  But wait, that means `startPreInit()` would call `updatePriorityState` for each host individually, which would be inefficient. Let me look at the description again...\n\n  Actually, looking at the description: \"29 additions, 39 deletions\" - this is a net reduction of 10 lines. The current code has the `hosts_added` accumulation pattern. The new code would be simpler.\n\n  Let me think about what `startPreInit()` would look like:\n  ```cpp\n  void Cluster::startPreInit() {\n    dns_cache_->iterateHostMap(\n        [&](absl::string_view host, const Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& info) {\n          addOrUpdateHost(host, info);\n        });\n    onPreInitComplete();\n  }\n  ```\n\n  And `onDnsHostAddOrUpdate()`:\n  ```cpp\n  void Cluster::onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(...) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Adding host info for {}\", host);\n    addOrUpdateHost(host, host_info);\n  }\n  ```\n\n  And `addOrUpdateHost()` would call `updatePriorityState()` directly.\n\n  But this means for `startPreInit()`, each host addition would trigger a separate `updatePriorityState()` call. That's less efficient but simpler. The description says \"updatePriorityState() is called directly within addOrUpdateHost()\" so this seems to be the intent.\n\n  Wait, but looking at the test:\n  ```cpp\n  if (!existing_hosts.empty()) {\n    EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(existing_hosts.size()), SizeIs(0)));\n  }\n  ```\n  This expects a single callback with all hosts. So we need to add:\n  ```cpp\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(1)));\n  ```\n  before the `update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate` call.\n\n  Also, the `InSequence s;` means the order matters. Currently:\n  ```cpp\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  // ... address update (no callback expected currently)\n  updateTestHostAddress(\"host1:0\", \"2.3.4.5\");\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  // ...\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(0), SizeIs(1)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostRemove(\"host1:0\");\n  ```\n\n  With the new approach:\n  ```cpp\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  // ... address update now triggers callback\n  updateTestHostAddress(\"host1:0\", \"2.3.4.5\");\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(1)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  // ...\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(0), SizeIs(1)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostRemove(\"host1:0\");\n  ```\n\n  Now let me think about the `initialize()` test setup. Currently:\n  ```cpp\n  if (!existing_hosts.empty()) {\n    EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(existing_hosts.size()), SizeIs(0)));\n  }\n  ```\n\n  With per-host updates, this would change to:\n  ```cpp\n  if (!existing_hosts.empty()) {\n    EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0))).Times(existing_hosts.size());\n  }\n  ```\n\n  Now let me think about the implementation of `addOrUpdateHost()`:\n\n  ```cpp\n  void Cluster::addOrUpdateHost(\n      absl::string_view host,\n      const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info) {\n    Upstream::HostVector hosts_added;\n    Upstream::HostVector hosts_removed;\n    {\n      absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n\n      const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\n      if (host_map_it != host_map_.end()) {\n        ASSERT(host_info == host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_);\n        ASSERT(host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_->address() !=\n               host_map_it->second.logical_host_->address());\n        ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"updating dfproxy cluster host address '{}'\", host);\n        hosts_removed.emplace_back(host_map_it->second.logical_host_);\n        host_map_.erase(host_map_it);\n      }\n\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"adding new dfproxy cluster host '{}'\", host);\n\n      auto new_host = std::make_shared<Upstream::LogicalHost>(\n          info(), std::string{host}, host_info->address(),\n          host_info->addressList(), dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_,\n          dummy_lb_endpoint_, nullptr, time_source_);\n      host_map_.try_emplace(host, host_info, new_host);\n      hosts_added.emplace_back(new_host);\n    }\n\n    updatePriorityState(hosts_added, hosts_removed);\n  }\n  ```\n\n  Wait, but there's a problem. The `updatePriorityState` function takes a reader lock on `host_map_lock_`. If we call it while holding the writer lock, we'd deadlock. But looking at the current code, `updatePriorityState` is called AFTER releasing the lock (the lock is released when the scope ends).\n\n  In the current code:\n  ```cpp\n  void Cluster::addOrUpdateHost(..., std::unique_ptr<Upstream::HostVector>& hosts_added) {\n    Upstream::LogicalHostSharedPtr emplaced_host;\n    {\n      absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n      // ... (lock released at end of scope)\n    }\n    // hosts_added is populated, caller calls updatePriorityState\n  }\n  ```\n\n  In the new code, we'd call `updatePriorityState` after releasing the lock:\n  ```cpp\n  void Cluster::addOrUpdateHost(...) {\n    Upstream::HostVector hosts_added;\n    Upstream::HostVector hosts_removed;\n    {\n      absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n      // ... modify host_map_\n      // populate hosts_added and hosts_removed\n    }\n    // lock released\n    updatePriorityState(hosts_added, hosts_removed);\n  }\n  ```\n\n  This is correct.\n\n  Now let me also think about the `startPreInit()` function. Currently:\n  ```cpp\n  void Cluster::startPreInit() {\n    std::unique_ptr<Upstream::HostVector> hosts_added;\n    dns_cache_->iterateHostMap(\n        [&](absl::string_view host, const Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& info) {\n          addOrUpdateHost(host, info, hosts_added);\n        });\n    if (hosts_added) {\n      updatePriorityState(*hosts_added, {});\n    }\n    onPreInitComplete();\n  }\n  ```\n\n  With the new approach:\n  ```cpp\n  void Cluster::startPreInit() {\n    dns_cache_->iterateHostMap(\n        [&](absl::string_view host, const Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& info) {\n          addOrUpdateHost(host, info);\n        });\n    onPreInitComplete();\n  }\n  ```\n\n  And `onDnsHostAddOrUpdate()`:\n  ```cpp\n  void Cluster::onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(...) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Adding host info for {}\", host);\n    addOrUpdateHost(host, host_info);\n  }\n  ```\n\n  Now for the test, the `initialize()` method calls `cluster_->initialize()` which calls `startPreInit()`. If there are existing hosts, each one would trigger a separate `updatePriorityState()` call, which would trigger a separate `onMemberUpdateCb` call.\n\n  So the test change in `initialize()` would be:\n  ```cpp\n  if (!existing_hosts.empty()) {\n    EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0))).Times(existing_hosts.size());\n  }\n  ```\n\n  Now let me look at the `BasicFlow` test more carefully. The `InSequence s;` is used, which means all expectations must be satisfied in order. With the new approach:\n\n  1. `EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));` - for adding host1\n  2. `update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);`\n  3. `updateTestHostAddress(\"host1:0\", \"2.3.4.5\");`\n  4. `EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(1)));` - for updating host1 (remove old, add new)\n  5. `update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);`\n  6. `EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(0), SizeIs(1)));` - for removing host1\n  7. `update_callbacks_->onDnsHostRemove(\"host1:0\");`\n\n  But wait, with `InSequence`, the EXPECT_CALL must come BEFORE the action. removed.\\n834:     return;\\n835:   }\\n836: \\n837:   // Since read_only_all_host_map_ may be shared by multiple threads, when the host set changes,\\n838:   // we cannot directly modify read_only_all_host_map_.\\n839:   if (mutable_cross_priority_host_map_ == nullptr) {\\n840:     // Copy old read only host map to mutable host map.\\n841:     mutable_cross_priority_host_map_ = std::make_shared<HostMap>(*const_cross_priority_host_map_);\\n842:   }\\n843: \\n844:   for (const auto& host : hosts_removed) {\\n845:     mutable_cross_priority_host_map_->erase(addressToString(host->address()));\\n846:   }\\n847: \\n848:   for (const auto& host : hosts_added) {\\n849:     mutable_cross_priority_host_map_->insert({addressToString(host->address()), host});\\n850:   }\\n851: }\\n852: \\n853: DeferredCreationCompatibleClusterTrafficStats\\n854: ClusterInfoImpl::generateStats(Stats::ScopeSharedPtr scope,\\n855:                                const ClusterTrafficStatNames& stat_names, bool defer_creation) {\\n856: \", \"url\": 13. think @ 2026-03-10T19:26:47.516809

Input

{
  "thought": "Now I have a clear picture. Let me plan the exact changes:\n\n1. **upstream_impl.cc**: Add debug logging in `updateCrossPriorityHostMap` for erase and insert operations.\n\n2. **cluster.h**: Change `addOrUpdateHost` signature to remove `hosts_added` parameter.\n\n3. **cluster.cc**: \n   - `startPreInit`: Remove `hosts_added` accumulation\n   - `addOrUpdateHost`: Remove `hosts_added` parameter, handle update case by removing old host and creating new one, call `updatePriorityState` directly\n   - `onDnsHostAddOrUpdate`: Simplify to just call `addOrUpdateHost`\n\n4. **cluster_test.cc**:\n   - In `initialize()`: Change to per-host callback expectation\n   - In `BasicFlow`: Add `EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(1)));` before the address update call\n\nNow let me think about the exact implementation of `addOrUpdateHost`:\n\n```cpp\nvoid Cluster::addOrUpdateHost(\n    absl::string_view host,\n    const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info) {\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_added;\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_removed;\n  {\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n\n    const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\n    if (host_map_it != host_map_.end()) {\n      ASSERT(host_info == host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_);\n      ASSERT(host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_->address() !=\n             host_map_it->second.logical_host_->address());\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"updating dfproxy cluster host address '{}'\", host);\n      hosts_removed.emplace_back(host_map_it->second.logical_host_);\n      host_map_.erase(host_map_it);\n    }\n\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"adding new dfproxy cluster host '{}'\", host);\n\n    auto new_host = std::make_shared<Upstream::LogicalHost>(\n        info(), std::string{host}, host_info->address(),\n        host_info->addressList(), dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_,\n        dummy_lb_endpoint_, nullptr, time_source_);\n    host_map_.try_emplace(host, host_info, new_host);\n    hosts_added.emplace_back(new_host);\n  }\n\n  updatePriorityState(hosts_added, hosts_removed);\n}\n```\n\nWait, but there's a subtle issue. The `try_emplace` uses `host` which is an `absl::string_view`. After erasing the old entry, we need to re-insert. Let me check if `try_emplace` works correctly here...\n\nActually, `try_emplace` with a key that doesn't exist will insert. Since we erased the old entry, the key doesn't exist anymore, so `try_emplace` will insert the new entry. This should work.\n\nBut wait, there's another issue. The `host_map_it` is invalidated after `host_map_.erase(host_map_it)`. But we're not using `host_map_it` after the erase, so that's fine.\n\nAlso, I need to make sure the `host` string_view is still valid after the erase. Since `host` is passed as a parameter (not stored in the map), it should be fine.\n\nLet me also think about the `startPreInit` function. With the new approach:\n\n```cpp\nvoid Cluster::startPreInit() {\n  dns_cache_->iterateHostMap(\n      [&](absl::string_view host, const Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& info) {\n        addOrUpdateHost(host, info);\n      });\n  onPreInitComplete();\n}\n```\n\nThis is simpler. Each host addition triggers a separate `updatePriorityState` call.\n\nAnd `onDnsHostAddOrUpdate`:\n```cpp\nvoid Cluster::onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\n    const std::string& host,\n    const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info) {\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Adding host info for {}\", host);\n  addOrUpdateHost(host, host_info);\n}\n```\n\nNow let me count the lines to verify the \"29 additions, 39 deletions\" claim:\n\nCurrent `startPreInit` (lines 99-110): 11 lines\nNew `startPreInit`: ~5 lines (saves ~6 lines)\n\nCurrent `addOrUpdateHost` (lines 238-295): ~57 lines\nNew `addOrUpdateHost`: ~25 lines (saves ~32 lines)\n\nCurrent `onDnsHostAddOrUpdate` (lines 297-308): ~11 lines\nNew `onDnsHostAddOrUpdate`: ~5 lines (saves ~6 lines)\n\nThat's roughly 44 deletions and ~35 additions. Close to the \"29 additions, 39 deletions\" estimate.\n\nNow let me also think about the test changes. The `BasicFlow` test needs:\n1. Add `EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(1)));` before the address update call\n\nAnd in `initialize()`:\n```cpp\nif (!existing_hosts.empty()) {\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0))).Times(existing_hosts.size());\n}\n```\n\nNow let me create the files. I'll start with the working directory.\n"
}

HostDescriptionImpl::HostDescriptionImpl(\\n406:     ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster, const std::string& hostname,\\n407:     Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr dest_address, MetadataConstSharedPtr metadata,\\n408:     const envoy::config::core::v3::Locality& locality,\\n409:     const envoy::config::endpoint::v3::Endpoint::HealthCheckConfig& health_check_config,\\n410:     uint32_t priority, TimeSource& time_source)\\n411:     : cluster_(cluster), hostname_(hostname),\\n412:       health_checks_hostname_(health_check_config.hostname()), address_(dest_address),\\n413:       canary_(Config::Metadata::metadataValue(metadata.get(),\\n414:                                               Config::MetadataFilters::get().ENVOY_LB,\\n415: cluster->statsScope().symbolTable()),\\n419:       priority_(priority),\\n420:       socket_factory_(resolveTransportSocketFactory(dest_address, metadata_.get())),\\n421:       creation_time_(time_source.monotonicTime()) {\\n422:   if (health_check_config.port_value() != 0 && dest_address->type() != Network::Address::Type::Ip) {\\n423:     // Setting the health check port to non-0 only works for IP-type addresses. Setting the port\\n424:     // for a pipe address is a misconfiguration. Throw an exception.\\n425:     throwEnvoyExceptionOrPanic(\\n426:         fmt::format(\\\"Invalid host configuration: non-zero port for non-IP address\\\"));\\n427:   }\\n428:   health_check_address_ = resolveHealthCheckAddress(health_check_config, dest_address);\\n429: }\\n430: \\n431: Network::UpstreamTransportSocketFactory& HostDescriptionImpl::resolveTransportSocketFactory(\\n432:     const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& dest_address,\\n433:     const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata* metadata) const {\\n434:   auto match = cluster_->transportSocketMatcher().resolve(metadata);\\n435:   match.stats_.total_match_count_.inc();\\n436:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"transport socket match, socket {} selected for host with address {}\\\",\\n437:             match.name_, dest_address ? dest_address->asString() : \\\"empty\\\");\\n438: \\n439:   return match.factory_;\\n440: }\\n441: \\n442: Host::CreateConnectionData HostImpl::createConnection(\\n443:     Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher, const Network::ConnectionSocket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\\n444:     Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr transport_socket_options) const {\\n445:   return createConnection(dispatcher, cluster(), address(), addressList(), transportSocketFactory(),\\n446:                           options, transport_socket_options, shared_from_this());\\n447: }\\n448: \\n449: void HostImpl::setEdsHealthFlag(envoy::config::core::v3::HealthStatus health_status) {\\n450:   // Clear all old EDS health flags first.\\n451:   HostImpl::healthFlagClear(Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_EDS_HEALTH);\\n452:   HostImpl::healthFlagClear(Host::HealthFlag::DEGRADED_EDS_HEALTH);\\n453: \\n454:   // Set the appropriate EDS health flag.\\n455:   switch (health_status) {\\n456:   case envoy::config::core::v3::UNHEALTHY:\\n457:     FALLTHRU;\\n458:   case envoy::config::core::v3::DRAINING:\\n459:     FALLTHRU;\\n460:   case envoy::config::core::v3::TIMEOUT:\\n461:     HostImpl::healthFlagSet(Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_EDS_HEALTH);\\n462:     break;\\n463:   case envoy::config::core::v3::DEGRADED:\\n464:     HostImpl::healthFlagSet(Host::HealthFlag::DEGRADED_EDS_HEALTH);\\n465:     break;\\n466:   default:\\n467:     break;\\n468:     // No health flags should be set.\\n469:   }\\n470: }\\n471: \\n472: Host::CreateConnectionData HostImpl::createHealthCheckConnection(\\n473:     Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,\\n474:     Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr transport_socket_options,\\n475:     const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata* metadata) const {\\n476: \\n477:   Network::UpstreamTransportSocketFactory& factory =\\n478:       (metadata != nullptr) ? resolveTransportSocketFactory(healthCheckAddress(), metadata)\\n479:                             : transportSocketFactory();\\n480:   return createConnection(dispatcher, cluster(), healthCheckAddress(), {}, factory, nullptr,\\n481:                           transport_socket_options, shared_from_this());\\n482: }\\n483: \\n484: Host::CreateConnectionData HostImpl::createConnection(\\n485:     Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher, const ClusterInfo& cluster,\\n486:     const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& address,\\n487:     const std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>& address_list,\\n488:     Network::UpstreamTransportSocketFactory& socket_factory,\\n489:     const Network::ConnectionSocket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\\n490:     Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr transport_socket_options,\\n491:     HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host) {\\n492:   auto source_address_selector = cluster.getUpstreamLocalAddressSelector();\\n493: \\n494:   Network::ClientConnectionPtr connection;\\n495:   // If the transport socket options indicate the connection should be\\n496:   // redirected to a proxy, create the TCP connection to the proxy's address not\\n497:   // the host's address.\\n498:   if (transport_socket_options && transport_socket_options->http11ProxyInfo().has_value()) {\\n499:     auto upstream_local_address =\\n500:         source_address_selector->getUpstreamLocalAddress(address, options);\\n501:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"Connecting to configured HTTP/1.1 proxy at {}\\\",\\n502:               transport_socket_options->http11ProxyInfo()->proxy_address->asString());\\n503:     connection = dispatcher.createClientConnection(\\n504:         transport_socket_options->http11ProxyInfo()->proxy_address, upstream_local_address.address_,\\n505:         socket_factory.createTransportSocket(transport_socket_options, host),\\n506:         upstream_local_address.socket_options_, transport_socket_options);\\n507:   } else if (address_list.size() > 1) {\\n508:     connection = std::make_unique<Network::HappyEyeballsConnectionImpl>(\\n509:         dispatcher, address_list, source_address_selector, socket_factory, transport_socket_options,\\n510:         host, options);\\n511:   } else {\\n512:     auto upstream_local_address =\\n513:         source_address_selector->getUpstreamLocalAddress(address, options);\\n514:     connection = dispatcher.createClientConnection(\\n515:         address, upstream_local_address.address_,\\n516:         socket_factory.createTransportSocket(transport_socket_options, host),\\n517:         upstream_local_address.socket_options_, transport_socket_options);\\n518:   }\\n519: \\n520:   connection->connectionInfoSetter().enableSettingInterfaceName(\\n521:       cluster.setLocalInterfaceNameOnUpstreamConnections());\\n522:   connection->setBufferLimits(cluster.perConnectionBufferLimitBytes());\\n523:   cluster.createNetworkFilterChain(*connection);\\n524:   return {std::move(connection), std::move(host)};\\n525: }\\n526: \\n527: void HostImpl::weight(uint32_t new_weight) { weight_ = std::max(1U, new_weight); }\\n528: \\n529: std::vector<HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr> HostsPerLocalityImpl::filter(\\n530:     const std::vector<std::function<bool(const Host&)>>& predicates) const {\\n531:   // We keep two lists: one for being able to mutate the clone and one for returning to the\\n532:   // caller. Creating them both at the start avoids iterating over the mutable values at the end\\n533:   // to convert them to a const pointer.\\n534:   std::vector<std::shared_ptr<HostsPerLocalityImpl>> mutable_clones;\\n535:   std::vector<HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr> filtered_clones;\\n536: \\n537:   mutable_clones.reserve(predicates.size());\\n538:   filtered_clones.reserve(predicates.size());\\n539:   for (size_t i = 0; i < predicates.size(); ++i) {\\n540:     mutable_clones.emplace_back(std::make_shared<HostsPerLocalityImpl>());\\n541:     filtered_clones.emplace_back(mutable_clones.back());\\n542:     mutable_clones.back()->local_ = local_;\\n543:   }\\n544: \\n545:   for (const auto& hosts_locality : hosts_per_locality_) {\\n546:     std::vector<HostVector> current_locality_hosts;\\n547:     current_locality_hosts.resize(predicates.size());\\n548: \\n549:     // Since # of hosts >> # of predicates, we iterate over the hosts in the outer loop.\\n550:     for (const auto& host : hosts_locality) {\\n551:       for (size_t i = 0; i < predicates.size(); ++i) {\\n552:         if (predicates[i](*host)) {\\n553:           current_locality_hosts[i].emplace_back(host);\\n554:         }\\n555:       }\\n556:     }\\n557: \\n558:     for (size_t i = 0; i < predicates.size(); ++i) {\\n559:       mutable_clones[i]->hosts_per_locality_.push_back(std::move(current_locality_hosts[i]));\\n560:     }\\n561:   }\\n562: \\n563:   return filtered_clones;\\n564: }\\n565: \\n566: void HostSetImpl::updateHosts(PrioritySet::UpdateHostsParams&& update_hosts_params,\\n567:                               LocalityWeightsConstSharedPtr locality_weights,\\n568:                               const HostVector& hosts_added, const HostVector& hosts_removed,\\n569:                               absl::optional<bool> weighted_priority_health,\\n570:                               absl::optional<uint32_t> overprovisioning_factor) {\\n571:   if (weighted_priority_health.has_value()) {\\n572:     weighted_priority_health_ = weighted_priority_health.value();\\n573:   }\\n574:   if (overprovisioning_factor.has_value()) {\\n575:     ASSERT(overprovisioning_factor.value() > 0);\\n576:     overprovisioning_factor_ = overprovisioning_factor.value();\\n577:   }\\n578:   hosts_ = std::move(update_hosts_params.hosts);\\n579:   healthy_hosts_ = std::move(update_hosts_params.healthy_hosts);\\n580:   degraded_hosts_ = std::move(update_hosts_params.degraded_hosts);\\n581:   excluded_hosts_ = std::move(update_hosts_params.excluded_hosts);\\n582:   hosts_per_locality_ = std::move(update_hosts_params.hosts_per_locality);\\n583:   healthy_hosts_per_locality_ = std::move(update_hosts_params.healthy_hosts_per_locality);\\n584:   degraded_hosts_per_locality_ = std::move(update_hosts_params.degraded_hosts_per_locality);\\n585:   excluded_hosts_per_locality_ = std::move(update_hosts_params.excluded_hosts_per_locality);\\n586:   locality_weights_ = std::move(locality_weights);\\n587: \\n588:   // TODO(ggreenway): implement `weighted_priority_health` support "{\"meta\": null, \"content\": [{\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"{\\\"content\\\":\\\"600: \\\\n601: void HostSetImpl::rebuildLocalityScheduler(\\\\n602:     std::unique_ptr\\\\u003cEdfScheduler\\\\u003cLocalityEntry\\\\u003e\\\\u003e\\\\u0026 locality_scheduler,\\\\n603:     std::vector\\\\u003cstd::shared_ptr\\\\u003cLocalityEntry\\\\u003e\\\\u003e\\\\u0026 locality_entries,\\\\n604:     const HostsPerLocality\\\\u0026 eligible_hosts_per_locality, const HostVector\\\\u0026 eligible_hosts,\\\\n605:     HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr all_hosts_per_locality,\\\\n606:     HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr excluded_hosts_per_locality,\\\\n607:     LocalityWeightsConstSharedPtr locality_weights, uint32_t overprovisioning_factor) {\\\\n608:   // Rebuild the locality scheduler by computing the effective weight of each\\\\n609:   // locality in this priority. The scheduler is reset by default, and is rebuilt only if we have\\\\n610:   // locality weights (i.e. using EDS) and there is at least one eligible host in this priority.\\\\n611:   //\\\\n612:   // We omit building a scheduler when there are zero eligible hosts in the priority as\\\\n613:   // all the localities will have zero effective weight. At selection time, we'll either select\\\\n614:   // from a different scheduler or there will be no available hosts in the priority. At that point\\\\n615:   // we'll rely on other mechanisms such as panic mode to select a host, none of which rely on the\\\\n616:   // scheduler.\\\\n617:   //\\\\n618:   // TODO(htuch): if the underlying locality index -\\\\u003e\\\\n619:   // envoy::config::core::v3::Locality hasn't changed in hosts_/healthy_hosts_/degraded_hosts_, we\\\\n620:   // could just update locality_weight_ without rebuilding. Similar to how host\\\\n621:   // level WRR works, we would age out the existing entries via picks and lazily\\\\n622:   // apply the new weights.\\\\n623:   locality_scheduler = nullptr;\\\\n624:   if (all_hosts_per_locality != nullptr \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 locality_weights != nullptr \\\\u0026\\\\u0026\\\\n625:       !locality_weights-\\\\u003eempty() \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !eligible_hosts.empty()) {\\\\n626:     locality_scheduler = std::make_unique\\\\u003cEdfScheduler\\\\u003cLocalityEntry\\\\u003e\\\\u003e();\\\\n627:     locality_entries.clear();\\\\n628:     for (uint32_t i = 0; i \\\\u003c all_hosts_per_locality-\\\\u003eget().size(); ++i) {\\\\n629:       const double effective_weight = effectiveLocalityWeight(\\\\n630:           i, eligible_hosts_per_locality, *excluded_hosts_per_locality, *all_hosts_per_locality,\\\\n631:           *locality_weights, overprovisioning_factor);\\\\n632:       if (effective_weight \\\\u003e 0) {\\\\n633:         locality_entries.emplace_back(std::make_shared\\\\u003cLocalityEntry\\\\u003e(i, effective_weight));\\\\n634:         locality_scheduler-\\\\u003eadd(effective_weight, locality_entries.back());\\\\n635:       }\\\\n636:     }\\\\n637:     // If all effective weights were zero, reset the scheduler.\\\\n638:     if (locality_scheduler-\\\\u003eempty()) {\\\\n639:       locality_scheduler = nullptr;\\\\n640:     }\\\\n641:   }\\\\n642: }\\\\n643: \\\\n644: absl::optional\\\\u003cuint32_t\\\\u003e HostSetImpl::chooseHealthyLocality() {\\\\n645:   return chooseLocality(healthy_locality_scheduler_.get());\\\\n646: }\\\\n647: \\\\n648: absl::optional\\\\u003cuint32_t\\\\u003e HostSetImpl::chooseDegradedLocality() {\\\\n649:   return chooseLocality(degraded_locality_scheduler_.get());\\\\n650: }\\\\n651: \\\\n652: absl::optional\\\\u003cuint32_t\\\\u003e\\\\n653: HostSetImpl::chooseLocality(EdfScheduler\\\\u003cLocalityEntry\\\\u003e* locality_scheduler) {\\\\n654:   if (locality_scheduler == nullptr) {\\\\n655:     return {};\\\\n656:   }\\\\n657:   const std::shared_ptr\\\\u003cLocalityEntry\\\\u003e locality = locality_scheduler-\\\\u003epickAndAdd(\\\\n658:       [](const LocalityEntry\\\\u0026 locality) { return locality.effective_weight_; });\\\\n659:   // We don't build a schedule if there are no weighted localities, so we should always succeed.\\\\n660:   ASSERT(locality != nullptr);\\\\n661:   // If we picked it before, its weight must have been positive.\\\\n662:   ASSERT(locality-\\\\u003eeffective_weight_ \\\\u003e 0);\\\\n663:   return locality-\\\\u003eindex_;\\\\n664: }\\\\n665: \\\\n666: PrioritySet::UpdateHostsParams\\\\n667: HostSetImpl::updateHostsParams(HostVectorConstSharedPtr hosts,\\\\n668:                                HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr hosts_per_locality,\\\\n669:                                HealthyHostVectorConstSharedPtr healthy_hosts,\\\\n670:                                HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr healthy_hosts_per_locality,\\\\n671:                                DegradedHostVectorConstSharedPtr degraded_hosts,\\\\n672:                                HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr }\\\\n706: \\\\n707: double HostSetImpl::effectiveLocalityWeight(uint32_t index,\\\\n708:                                             const HostsPerLocality\\\\u0026 eligible_hosts_per_locality,\\\\n709:                                             const HostsPerLocality\\\\u0026 excluded_hosts_per_locality,\\\\n710:                                             const HostsPerLocality\\\\u0026 all_hosts_per_locality,\\\\n711:                                             const LocalityWeights\\\\u0026 locality_weights,\\\\n712:                                             uint32_t overprovisioning_factor) {\\\\n713:   const auto\\\\u0026 locality_eligible_hosts = eligible_hosts_per_locality.get()[index];\\\\n714:   const uint32_t excluded_count = excluded_hosts_per_locality.get().size() \\\\u003e index\\\\n715:                                       ? excluded_hosts_per_locality.get()[index].size()\\\\n716:                                       : 0;\\\\n717:   const auto host_count = all_hosts_per_locality.get()[index].size() - excluded_count;\\\\n718:   if (host_count == 0) {\\\\n719:     return 0.0;\\\\n720:   }\\\\n721:   const double locality_availability_ratio = 1.0 * locality_eligible_hosts.size() / host_count;\\\\n722:   const uint32_t weight = locality_weights[index];\\\\n723:   // Availability ranges from 0-1.0, and is the ratio of eligible hosts to total hosts, modified\\\\n724:   // by the overprovisioning factor.\\\\n725:   const double effective_locality_availability_ratio =\\\\n726:       std::min(1.0, (overprovisioning_factor / 100.0) * locality_availability_ratio);\\\\n727:   return weight * effective_locality_availability_ratio;\\\\n728: }\\\\n729: \\\\n730: const HostSet\\\\u0026\\\\n731: PrioritySetImpl::getOrCreateHostSet(uint32_t priority,\\\\n732:                                     absl::optional\\\\u003cbool\\\\u003e weighted_priority_health,\\\\n733:                                     absl::optional\\\\u003cuint32_t\\\\u003e overprovisioning_factor) {\\\\n734:   if (host_sets_.size() \\\\u003c priority + 1) {\\\\n735:     for (size_t i = host_sets_.size(); i \\\\u003c= priority; ++i) {\\\\n736:       HostSetImplPtr host_set = createHostSet(i, weighted_priority_health, overprovisioning_factor);\\\\n737:       host_sets_priority_update_cbs_.push_back(\\\\n738: hosts_removed) {\\\\n740:             runReferenceUpdateCallbacks(priority, hosts_added, hosts_removed);\\\\n741:           }));\\\\n742:       host_sets_.push_back(std::move(host_set));\\\\n743: update_hosts_params,\\\\n749:                                   LocalityWeightsConstSharedPtr locality_weights,\\\\n750:                                   const HostVector\\\\u0026 hosts_added, const HostVector\\\\u0026 hosts_removed,\\\\n751:                                   absl::optional\\\\u003cbool\\\\u003e weighted_priority_health,\\\\n752:                                   absl::optional\\\\u003cuint32_t\\\\u003e overprovisioning_factor,\\\\n753:                                   HostMapConstSharedPtr cross_priority_host_map) {\\\\n754:   // Update cross priority host map first. In this way, when the update callbacks of the priority\\\\n755:   // set are executed, the latest host map can always be obtained.\\\\n756:   if (cross_priority_host_map != nullptr) {\\\\n757:     const_cross_priority_host_map_ = std::move(cross_priority_host_map);\\\\n758:   }\\\\n759: \\\\n760:   // Ensure that we have a HostSet for the given priority.\\\\n761:   getOrCreateHostSet(priority, weighted_priority_health, overprovisioning_factor);\\\\n762:   static_cast\\\\u003cHostSetImpl*\\\\u003e(host_sets_[priority].get())\\\\n763: \\\\n766:   if (!batch_update_) {\\\\n767:     runUpdateCallbacks(hosts_added, hosts_removed);\\\\n768:   }\\\\n769: }\\\\n770: \\\\n771: void PrioritySetImpl::batchHostUpdate(BatchUpdateCb\\\\u0026 callback) {\\\\n772:   BatchUpdateScope scope(*this);\\\\n773: \\\\n774:   // We wrap the update call with a lambda that tracks all the hosts that have been added/removed.\\\\n775:   callback.batchUpdate(scope);\\\\n776: \\\\n777:   // Now that all the updates have been complete, we can compute the diff.\\\\n778:   HostVector net_hosts_added = filterHosts(scope.all_hosts_added_, scope.all_hosts_removed_);\\\\n779:   HostVector net_hosts_removed = filterHosts(scope.all_hosts_removed_, scope.all_hosts_added_);\\\\n780: \\\\n781:   runUpdateCallbacks(net_hosts_added, net_hosts_removed);\\\\n782: }\\\\n783: \\\\n784: void PrioritySetImpl::BatchUpdateScope::updateHosts(\\\\n785:     uint32_t priority, PrioritySet::UpdateHostsParams\\\\u0026\\\\u0026 update_hosts_params,\\\\n786:     LocalityWeightsConstSharedPtr locality_weights, const HostVector\\\\u0026 hosts_added,\\\\n787:     const HostVector\\\\u0026 hosts_removed, absl::optional\\\\u003cbool\\\\u003e weighted_priority_health,\\\\n788:     absl::optional\\\\u003cuint32_t\\\\u003e overprovisioning_factor) {\\\\n789:   // We assume that each call updates a different priority.\\\\n790:   ASSERT(priorities_.find(priority) == priorities_.end());\\\\n791:   priorities_.insert(priority);\\\\n792: \\\\n793:   for (const auto\\\\u0026 host : hosts_added) {\\\\n794:     all_hosts_added_.insert(host);\\\\n795:   }\\\\n796: \\\\n797:   for (const auto\\\\u0026 host : hosts_removed) {\\\\n798:     all_hosts_removed_.insert(host);\\\\n799:   }\\\\n800: \\\\n801:   parent_.updateHosts(priority, std::move(update_hosts_params), locality_weights, hosts_added,\\\\n802:                       hosts_removed, weighted_priority_health, overprovisioning_factor);\\\\n803: }\\\\n804: \\\\n805: void MainPrioritySetImpl::updateHosts(uint32_t priority, UpdateHostsParams\\\\u0026\\\\u0026 update_hosts_params,\\\\n806:                                       LocalityWeightsConstSharedPtr locality_weights,\\\\n807:                                       const HostVector\\\\u0026 hosts_added,\\\\n808:                                       const HostVector\\\\u0026 hosts_removed,\\\\n809:                                       absl::optional\\\\u003cbool\\\\u003e weighted_priority_health,\\\\n810:                                       absl::optional\\\\u003cuint32_t\\\\u003e overprovisioning_factor,\\\\n811:                                       HostMapConstSharedPtr cross_priority_host_map) {\\\\n812: \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"600: \\n601: void HostSetImpl::rebuildLocalityScheduler(\\n602:     std::unique_ptr<EdfScheduler<LocalityEntry>>& locality_scheduler,\\n603:     std::vector<std::shared_ptr<LocalityEntry>>& locality_entries,\\n604:     const HostsPerLocality& eligible_hosts_per_locality, const HostVector& eligible_hosts,\\n605:     HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr all_hosts_per_locality,\\n606:     HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr excluded_hosts_per_locality,\\n607:     LocalityWeightsConstSharedPtr locality_weights, uint32_t overprovisioning_factor) {\\n608:   // Rebuild the locality scheduler by computing the effective weight of each\\n609:   // locality in this priority. The scheduler is reset by default, and is rebuilt only if we have\\n610:   // locality weights (i.e. using EDS) and there is at least one eligible host in this priority.\\n611:   //\\n612:   // We omit building a scheduler when there are zero eligible hosts in the priority as\\n613:   // all the localities will have zero effective weight. At selection time, we'll either select\\n614:   // from a different scheduler or there will be no available hosts in the priority. At that point\\n615:   // we'll rely on other mechanisms such as panic mode to select a host, none of which rely on the\\n616:   // scheduler.\\n617:   //\\n618:   // TODO(htuch): if the underlying locality index ->\\n619:   // envoy::config::core::v3::Locality hasn't changed in hosts_/healthy_hosts_/degraded_hosts_, we\\n620:   // could just update locality_weight_ without rebuilding. Similar to how host\\n621:   // level WRR works, we would age out the existing entries via picks and lazily\\n622:   // apply the new weights.\\n623:   locality_scheduler = nullptr;\\n624:   if (all_hosts_per_locality != nullptr && locality_weights != nullptr &&\\n625:       !locality_weights->empty() && !eligible_hosts.empty()) {\\n626:     locality_scheduler = std::make_unique<EdfScheduler<LocalityEntry>>();\\n627:     locality_entries.clear();\\n628:     for (uint32_t i = 0; i < all_hosts_per_locality->get().size(); ++i) {\\n629:       const double effective_weight = effectiveLocalityWeight(\\n630:           i, eligible_hosts_per_locality, *excluded_hosts_per_locality, *all_hosts_per_locality,\\n631:           *locality_weights, overprovisioning_factor);\\n632:       if (effective_weight > 0) {\\n633:         locality_entries.emplace_back(std::make_shared<LocalityEntry>(i, effective_weight));\\n634:         locality_scheduler->add(effective_weight, locality_entries.back());\\n635:       }\\n636:     }\\n637:     // If all effective weights were zero, reset the scheduler.\\n638:     if (locality_scheduler->empty()) {\\n639:       locality_scheduler = nullptr;\\n640:     }\\n641:   }\\n642: }\\n643: \\n644: absl::optional<uint32_t> HostSetImpl::chooseHealthyLocality() {\\n645:   return chooseLocality(healthy_locality_scheduler_.get());\\n646: }\\n647: \\n648: absl::optional<uint32_t> HostSetImpl::chooseDegradedLocality() {\\n649:   return chooseLocality(degraded_locality_scheduler_.get());\\n650: }\\n651: \\n652: absl::optional<uint32_t>\\n653: HostSetImpl::chooseLocality(EdfScheduler<LocalityEntry>* locality_scheduler) {\\n654:   if (locality_scheduler == nullptr) {\\n655:     return {};\\n656:   }\\n657:   const std::shared_ptr<LocalityEntry> locality = locality_scheduler->pickAndAdd(\\n658:       [](const LocalityEntry& locality) { return locality.effective_weight_; });\\n659:   // We don't build a schedule if there are no weighted localities, so we should always succeed.\\n660:   ASSERT(locality != nullptr);\\n661:   // If we picked it before, its weight must have been positive.\\n662:   ASSERT(locality->effective_weight_ > 0);\\n663:   return locality->index_;\\n664: }\\n665: \\n666: PrioritySet::UpdateHostsParams\\n667: HostSetImpl::updateHostsParams(HostVectorConstSharedPtr hosts,\\n668:                                HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr hosts_per_locality,\\n669:                                HealthyHostVectorConstSharedPtr healthy_hosts,\\n670:                                HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr healthy_hosts_per_locality,\\n671:                                DegradedHostVectorConstSharedPtr degraded_hosts,\\n672:                                HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr degraded_hosts_per_locality,\\n673:                                ExcludedHostVectorConstSharedPtr excluded_hosts,\\n674:                                HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr excluded_hosts_per_locality) {\\n675:   return PrioritySet::UpdateHostsParams{std::move(hosts),\\n676:                                         std::move(healthy_hosts),\\n677:                                         std::move(degraded_hosts),\\n678:                                         std::move(excluded_hosts),\\n679:                                         std::move(hosts_per_locality),\\n680:                                         std::move(healthy_hosts_per_locality),\\n681:                                         std::move(degraded_hosts_per_locality),\\n682:                                         std::move(excluded_hosts_per_locality)};\\n683: }\\n684: \\n685: PrioritySet::UpdateHostsParams HostSetImpl::updateHostsParams(const HostSet& host_set) {\\n686:   return updateHostsParams(host_set.hostsPtr(), host_set.hostsPerLocalityPtr(),\\n687:                            host_set.healthyHostsPtr(), host_set.healthyHostsPerLocalityPtr(),\\n688:                            host_set.degradedHostsPtr(), host_set.degradedHostsPerLocalityPtr(),\\n689:                            host_set.excludedHostsPtr(), host_set.excludedHostsPerLocalityPtr());\\n690: }\\n691: PrioritySet::UpdateHostsParams\\n692: HostSetImpl::partitionHosts(HostVectorConstSharedPtr hosts,\\n693:                             HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr hosts_per_locality) {\\n694:   auto partitioned_hosts = ClusterImplBase::partitionHostList(*hosts);\\n695:   auto healthy_degraded_excluded_hosts_per_locality =\\n696:       ClusterImplBase::partitionHostsPerLocality(*hosts_per_locality);\\n697: \\n698:   return updateHostsParams(std::move(hosts), std::move(hosts_per_locality),\\n699:                            std::move(std::get<0>(partitioned_hosts)),\\n700:                            std::move(std::get<0>(healthy_degraded_excluded_hosts_per_locality)),\\n701:                            std::move(std::get<1>(partitioned_hosts)),\\n702:                            std::move(std::get<1>(healthy_degraded_excluded_hosts_per_locality)),\\n703:                            std::move(std::get<2>(partitioned_hosts)),\\n704:                            std::move(std::get<2>(healthy_degraded_excluded_hosts_per_locality)));\\n705: }\\n706: \\n707: double HostSetImpl::effectiveLocalityWeight(uint32_t index,\\n708:                                             const HostsPerLocality& eligible_hosts_per_locality,\\n709:                                             const HostsPerLocality& excluded_hosts_per_locality,\\n710:                                             const HostsPerLocality& all_hosts_per_locality,\\n711:                                             const LocalityWeights& locality_weights,\\n712:                                             uint32_t overprovisioning_factor) {\\n713:   const auto& locality_eligible_hosts = eligible_hosts_per_locality.get()[index];\\n714:   const uint32_t excluded_count = excluded_hosts_per_locality.get().size() > index\\n715:                                       ? excluded_hosts_per_locality.get()[index].size()\\n716:                                       : 0;\\n717:   const auto host_count = all_hosts_per_locality.get()[index].size() - excluded_count;\\n718:   if (host_count == 0) {\\n719:     return 0.0;\\n720:   }\\n721:   const double locality_availability_ratio = 1.0 * locality_eligible_hosts.size() / host_count;\\n722:   const uint32_t weight = locality_weights[index];\\n723:   // Availability ranges from 0-1.0, and is the ratio of eligible hosts to total hosts, modified\\n724:   // by the overprovisioning factor.\\n725:   const double effective_locality_availability_ratio =\\n726:       std::min(1.0, (overprovisioning_factor / 100.0) * locality_availability_ratio);\\n727:   return weight * effective_locality_availability_ratio;\\n728: }\\n729: \\n730: const HostSet&\\n731: PrioritySetImpl::getOrCreateHostSet(uint32_t priority,\\n732:                                     absl::optional<bool> weighted_priority_health,\\n733:                                     absl::optional<uint32_t> overprovisioning_factor) {\\n734:   if (host_sets_.size() < priority + 1) {\\n735:     for (size_t i = host_sets_.size(); i <= priority; ++i) {\\n736:       HostSetImplPtr host_set = createHostSet(i, weighted_priority_health, overprovisioning_factor);\\n737:       host_sets_priority_update_cbs_.push_back(\\n738:           host_set->addPriorityUpdateCb([this](uint32_t priority, const HostVector& hosts_added,\\n739:                                                const HostVector& hosts_removed) {\\n740:             runReferenceUpdateCallbacks(priority, hosts_added, hosts_removed);\\n741:           }));\\n742:       host_sets_.push_back(std::move(host_set));\\n743:     }\\n744:   }\\n745:   return *host_sets_[priority];\\n746: }\\n747: \\n748: void PrioritySetImpl::updateHosts(uint32_t priority, UpdateHostsParams&& update_hosts_params,\\n749:                                   LocalityWeightsConstSharedPtr locality_weights,\\n750:                                   const HostVector& hosts_added, const HostVector& hosts_removed,\\n751:                                   absl::optional<bool> weighted_priority_health,\\n752:                                   absl::optional<uint32_t> overprovisioning_factor,\\n753:                                   HostMapConstSharedPtr cross_priority_host_map) {\\n754:   // Update cross priority host map first. In this way, when the update callbacks of the priority\\n755:   // set are executed, the latest host map can always be obtained.\\n756:   if (cross_priority_host_map != nullptr) {\\n757:     const_cross_priority_host_map_ = std::move(cross_priority_host_map);\\n758:   }\\n759: \\n760:   // Ensure that we have a HostSet for the given priority.\\n761:   getOrCreateHostSet(priority, weighted_priority_health, overprovisioning_factor);\\n762:   static_cast<HostSetImpl*>(host_sets_[priority].get())\\n763:       ->updateHosts(std::move(update_hosts_params), std::move(locality_weights), hosts_added,\\n764:                     hosts_removed, weighted_priority_health, overprovisioning_factor);\\n765: \\n766:   if (!batch_update_) {\\n767:     runUpdateCallbacks(hosts_added, hosts_removed);\\n768:   }\\n769: }\\n770: \\n771: void PrioritySetImpl::batchHostUpdate(BatchUpdateCb& callback) {\\n772:   BatchUpdateScope scope(*this);\\n773: \\n774:   // We wrap the update call with a lambda that tracks all the hosts that have been added/removed.\\n775:   callback.batchUpdate(scope);\\n776: \\n777:   // Now that all the updates have been complete, we can compute the diff.\\n778:   HostVector net_hosts_added = filterHosts(scope.all_hosts_added_, scope.all_hosts_removed_);\\n779:   HostVector net_hosts_removed = filterHosts(scope.all_hosts_removed_, scope.all_hosts_added_);\\n780: \\n781:   runUpdateCallbacks(net_hosts_added, net_hosts_removed);\\n782: }\\n783: \\n784: void PrioritySetImpl::BatchUpdateScope::updateHosts(\\n785:     uint32_t priority, PrioritySet::UpdateHostsParams&& update_hosts_params,\\n786:     LocalityWeightsConstSharedPtr locality_weights, const HostVector& hosts_added,\\n787:     const HostVector& hosts_removed, absl::optional<bool> weighted_priority_health,\\n788:     absl::optional<uint32_t> overprovisioning_factor) {\\n789:   // We assume hosts_added) {\\n794:     all_hosts_added_.insert(host);\\n795:   }\\n796: \\n797:   for (const auto& host : hosts_removed) {\\n798:     all_hosts_removed_.insert(host);\\n799:   }\\n800: \\n801: The verifier compares my workspace files against the expected ground-truth diff. The scoring is:\n- 0.35 * file_recall: Did I modify the right files?\n- 0.45 * line_recall: Did I add/remove the right lines?\n- 0.20 * line_precision: Are my added lines correct?\n\nThe key insight is that the verifier computes a diff between my workspace files and the original repository files. If I create a file that only contains the changed section (not the full file), the diff would show many deletions (all the lines I didn't include) and additions (the changed lines). This would hurt my line_precision score.\n\nHowever, if I create the full file with only the specific changes, the diff would only show the expected changes.\n\nFor upstream_impl.cc, the change is small (adding 2 debug log lines). The file is very large. Reading the entire file would take many API calls.\n\nLet me think of a better approach:\n1. I could create a script that reads the original file and applies the changes\n2. Or I could use a different strategy\n\nActually, wait. Let me re-read the instructions: \"create or edit only the files you need directly in the working directory using repository-relative paths.\"\n\nThe key word is \"edit\". I can create a file that is a modified version of the original. But since I can't run git commands to clone the repo, I need to create the files from scratch.\n\nFor large files like upstream_impl.cc, I need to read the full content and create a modified version. This is time-consuming but necessary.\n\nAlternatively, I could focus on the files that have the most impact on the score:\n- cluster.cc (most changes)\n- cluster.h (signature change)\n- cluster_test.cc (test changes)\n- upstream_impl.cc (small change)\n\nThe file_recall score (0.35) requires me to modify all 4 files. The line_recall score (0.45) requires me to add/remove the right lines. The line_precision score (0.20) requires my added lines to be correct.\n\nFor upstream_impl.cc, the change is small (2 debug log lines). If I create the full file with the change, I get full credit for that file. If I don't create it, I lose 0.35/4 = 0.0875 of the file_recall score.\n\nLet me prioritize:\n1. Create cluster.cc (most changes, highest impact)\n2. Create cluster.h (signature change)\n3. Create cluster_test.cc (test changes)\n4. Create upstream_impl.cc (small change, but still needed for file_recall)\n\nFor upstream_impl.cc, I need to read the rest of the file. Let me continue reading.\n\nActually, let me think about this more carefully. The file is at least 1100 lines. Reading it in chunks of 200 lines would take at least 6 more API calls. That's a lot.\n\nLet me check if there's a way to get the file size or the total number of lines.\n\nActually, I think the most efficient approach is to:\n1. Create the 3 smaller files (cluster.cc, cluster.h, cluster_test.cc) first\n2. Then read the rest of upstream_impl.cc and create it\n\nLet me start with the files I already have full content for.\n"

connection_;       // Not a ref to allow assignment in remove().\n    };\n    struct LookupKey {\n      const std::vector<uint8_t> hash_key_;\n      const Network::Address::Instance& peer_address_;\n      bool operator==(const LookupKey& rhs) const {\n        return std::tie(hash_key_, peer_address_) == std::tie(rhs.hash_key_, rhs.peer_address_);\n      }\n    };\n    struct LookupKeyHash {\n      size_t operator()(const LookupKey& lookup_key) const {\n        return std::hash<std::string>{}(lookup_key.peer_address_.asString());\n      }\n    };\n\n    absl::flat_hash_map<LookupKey, std::vector<ConnectionInfo>, LookupKeyHash> connection_info_map_;\n\n    const Cluster& cluster_;\n  };\n\n  class LoadBalancerFactory : public Upstream::LoadBalancerFactory {\n  public:\n
