\"envoy/extensions/clusters/dynamic_forward_proxy/v3/cluster.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/extensions/clusters/dynamic_forward_proxy/v3/cluster.pb.validate.h\"\n#include \"envoy/router/string_accessor.h\"\n#include \"envoy/stream_info/uint32_accessor.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n#include \"source/common/network/transport_socket_options_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/router/string_accessor_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/stream_info/uint32_accessor_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/extensions/common/dynamic_forward_proxy/dns_cache_manager_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.h\"\n#include \"source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/utility.h\"\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Extensions {\nnamespace Clusters {\nnamespace DynamicForwardProxy {\n\nnamespace {\nconstexpr absl::string_view DynamicHostFilterStateKey = \"envoy.upstream.dynamic_host\";\nconstexpr absl::string_view DynamicPortFilterStateKey = \"envoy.upstream.dynamic_port\";\n\nclass DynamicHostObjectFactory : public StreamInfo::FilterState::ObjectFactory {\npublic:\n  std::string name() const override { return std::string(DynamicHostFilterStateKey); }\n  std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::FilterState::Object>\n  createFromBytes(absl::string_view data) const override {\n    return std::make_unique<Router::StringAccessorImpl>(data);\n  }\n};\nclass DynamicPortObjectFactory : public StreamInfo::FilterState::ObjectFactory {\npublic:\n  std::string name() const override { return std::string(DynamicPortFilterStateKey); }\n  std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::FilterState::Object>\n  createFromBytes(absl::string_view data) const override {\n    uint32_t port = 0;\n    if (absl::SimpleAtoi(data, &port)) {\n      return std::make_unique<StreamInfo::UInt32AccessorImpl>(port);\n    }\n    return nullptr;\n  }\n};\n\n} // namespace\n\nREGISTER_FACTORY(DynamicHostObjectFactory, StreamInfo::FilterState::ObjectFactory);\nREGISTER_FACTORY(DynamicPortObjectFactory, StreamInfo::FilterState::ObjectFactory);\n\nCluster::Cluster(\n    const envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster& cluster,\n    Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCacheSharedPtr&& cache,\n    const envoy::extensions::clusters::dynamic_forward_proxy::v3::ClusterConfig& config,\n    Upstream::ClusterFactoryContext& context,\n    Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCacheManagerSharedPtr&& cache_manager)\n    : Upstream::BaseDynamicClusterImpl(cluster, context),\n      dns_cache_manager_(std::move(cache_manager)), dns_cache_(std::move(cache)),\n      update_callbacks_handle_(dns_cache_->addUpdateCallbacks(*this)),\n      local_info_(context.serverFactoryContext().localInfo()),\n      main_thread_dispatcher_(context.serverFactoryContext().mainThreadDispatcher()),\n      orig_cluster_config_(cluster),\n      allow_coalesced_connections_(config.allow_coalesced_connections()),\n      cm_(context.clusterManager()), max_sub_clusters_(PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(\n                                         config.sub_clusters_config(), max_sub_clusters, 1024)),\n      sub_cluster_ttl_(\n          PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(config.sub_clusters_config(), sub_cluster_ttl, 300000)),\n      sub_cluster_lb_policy_(config.sub_clusters_config().lb_policy()),\n      enable_sub_cluster_(config.has_sub_clusters_config()) {\n\n  if (enable_sub_cluster_) {\n    idle_timer_ = main_thread_dispatcher_.createTimer([this]() { checkIdleSubCluster(); });\n    idle_timer_->enableTimer(sub_cluster_ttl_);\n  }\n}\n\nCluster::~Cluster() {\n  if (enable_sub_cluster_) {\n    idle_timer_->disableTimer();\n    idle_timer_.reset();\n  }\n  if (cm_.isShutdown()) {\n    return;\n  }\n  // Should remove all sub clusters, otherwise, might be memory leaking.\n  // This lock is useless, just make compiler happy.\n  absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&cluster_map_lock_};\n  for (auto it = cluster_map_.cbegin(); it != cluster_map_.cend();) {\n    auto cluster_name = it->first;\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' removing from cluster_map & cluster manager\", cluster_name);\n    cluster_map_.erase(it++);\n    cm_.removeCluster(cluster_name);\n  }\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::startPreInit() {\n  // If we are attaching to a pre-populated cache we need to initialize our hosts.\n  dns_cache_->iterateHostMap(\n      [&](absl::string_view host, const Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& info) {\n        addOrUpdateHost(host, info);\n      });\n  onPreInitComplete();\n}\n\nbool Cluster::touch(const std::string& cluster_name) {\n  absl::ReaderMutexLock lock{&cluster_map_lock_};\n  const auto cluster_it = cluster_map_.find(cluster_name);\n  if (cluster_it != cluster_map_.end()) {\n    cluster_it->second->touch();\n    return true;\n  }\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' has been removed while touching\", cluster_name);\n  return false;\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::checkIdleSubCluster() {\n  ASSERT(main_thread_dispatcher_.isThreadSafe());\n  {\n    // TODO: try read lock first.\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&cluster_map_lock_};\n    for (auto it = cluster_map_.cbegin(); it != cluster_map_.cend();) {\n      if (it->second->checkIdle()) {\n        auto cluster_name = it->first;\n        ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' removing from cluster_map & cluster manager\", cluster_name);\n        cluster_map_.erase(it++);\n        cm_.removeCluster(cluster_name);\n      } else {\n        ++it;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  idle_timer_->enableTimer(sub_cluster_ttl_);\n}\n\nstd::pair<bool, absl::optional<envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster>>\nCluster::createSubClusterConfig(const std::string& cluster_name, const std::string& host,\n                                const int port) {\n  {\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&cluster_map_lock_};\n    const auto cluster_it = cluster_map_.find(cluster_name);\n    if (cluster_it != cluster_map_.end()) {\n      cluster_it->second->touch();\n      return std::make_pair(true, absl::nullopt);\n    }\n    if (cluster_map_.size() >= max_sub_clusters_) {\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' create failed due to max sub cluster limitation\",\n                cluster_name);\n      return std::make_pair(false, absl::nullopt);\n    }\n    cluster_map_.emplace(cluster_name, std::make_shared<ClusterInfo>(cluster_name, *this));\n  }\n\n  // Inherit configuration from the parent DFP cluster.\n  envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster config = orig_cluster_config_;\n\n  // Overwrite the type.\n  config.set_name(cluster_name);\n  config.clear_cluster_type();\n  config.set_lb_policy(sub_cluster_lb_policy_);\n  config.set_type(\n      envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster_DiscoveryType::Cluster_DiscoveryType_STRICT_DNS);\n\n  // Set endpoint.\n  auto load_assignments = config.mutable_load_assignment();\n  load_assignments->set_cluster_name(cluster_name);\n  load_assignments->clear_endpoints();\n\n  auto socket_address = load_assignments->add_endpoints()\n                            ->add_lb_endpoints()\n                            ->mutable_endpoint()\n                            ->mutable_address()\n                            ->mutable_socket_address();\n  socket_address->set_address(host);\n  socket_address->set_port_value(port);\n\n  return std::make_pair(true, absl::make_optional(config));\n}\n\nUpstream::HostConstSharedPtr Cluster::chooseHost(absl::string_view host,\n                                                 Upstream::LoadBalancerContext* context) const {\n  uint16_t default_port = 80;\n  if (info_->transportSocketMatcher().resolve(nullptr).factory_.implementsSecureTransport()) {\n    default_port = 443;\n  }\n\n  const auto host_attributes = Http::Utility::parseAuthority(host);\n  auto dynamic_host = std::string(host_attributes.host_);\n  auto port = host_attributes.port_.value_or(default_port);\n\n  // cluster name is prefix + host + port\n  auto cluster_name = \"DFPCluster:\" + dynamic_host + \":\" + std::to_string(port);\n\n  // try again to get the sub cluster.\n  auto cluster = cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(cluster_name);\n  if (cluster == nullptr) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' get thread local failed, too short ttl?\", cluster_name);\n    return nullptr;\n  }\n\n  return cluster->loadBalancer().chooseHost(context);\n}\n\nCluster::ClusterInfo::ClusterInfo(std::string cluster_name, Cluster& parent)\n    : cluster_name_(cluster_name), parent_(parent) {\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' ClusterInfo created\", cluster_name_);\n  touch();\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::ClusterInfo::touch() {\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' updating last used time\", cluster_name_);\n  last_used_time_ = parent_.time_source_.monotonicTime().time_since_epoch();\n}\n\n// checkIdle run in the main thread.\nbool Cluster::ClusterInfo::checkIdle() {\n  ASSERT(parent_.main_thread_dispatcher_.isThreadSafe());\n\n  const std::chrono::steady_clock::duration now_duration =\n      parent_.main_thread_dispatcher_.timeSource().monotonicTime().time_since_epoch();\n  auto last_used_time = last_used_time_.load();\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' TTL check: now={} last_used={} TTL {}\", cluster_name_,\n            now_duration.count(), last_used_time.count(), parent_.sub_cluster_ttl_.count());\n\n  if ((now_duration - last_used_time) > parent_.sub_cluster_ttl_) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' TTL expired\", cluster_name_);\n    return true;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::addOrUpdateHost(\n    absl::string_view host,\n    const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info) {\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_added;\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_removed;\n  {\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n\n    // NOTE: Right now we allow a DNS cache to be shared between multiple clusters. Though we have\n    // connection/request circuit breakers on the cluster, we don't have any way to control the\n    // maximum hosts on a cluster. We currently assume that host data shared via shared pointer is\n    // a marginal memory cost above that already used by connections and requests, so relying on\n    // connection/request circuit breakers is sufficient. We may have to revisit this in the\n    // future.\n    const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\n    if (host_map_it != host_map_.end()) {\n      // If the address has changed, remove the old host and add a new one so that the\n      // cross-priority host map is updated correctly. The old address would otherwise leak\n      // from the cross-priority host map when the host's TTL expires.\n      //\n      // TODO(mattklein123): Right now the dynamic forward proxy / DNS cache works similar to how\n      //                     logical DNS works, meaning that we only store a single address per\n      //                     resolution. It would not be difficult to also expose strict DNS\n      //                     semantics, meaning the cache would expose multiple addresses and the\n      //                     cluster would create multiple logical hosts based on those addresses.\n      //                     We will leave this is a follow up depending on need.\n      ASSERT(host_info == host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_);\n      ASSERT(host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_->address() !=\n             host_map_it->second.logical_host_->address());\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"updating dfproxy cluster host address '{}'\", host);\n      hosts_removed.emplace_back(host_map_it->second.logical_host_);\n      host_map_.erase(host_map_it);\n    }\n\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"adding new dfproxy cluster host '{}'\", host);\n\n    auto new_host = std::make_shared<Upstream::LogicalHost>(\n        info(), std::string{host}, host_info->address(), host_info->addressList(),\n        dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_, dummy_lb_endpoint_, nullptr, time_source_);\n    host_map_.try_emplace(host, host_info, new_host);\n    hosts_added.emplace_back(new_host);\n  }\n\n  updatePriorityState(hosts_added, hosts_removed);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\n    const std::string& host,\n    const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info) {\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Adding host info for {}\", host);\n\n  addOrUpdateHost(host, host_info);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::updatePriorityState(const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_added,\n                                  const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_removed) {\n  Upstream::PriorityStateManager priority_state_manager(*this, local_info_, nullptr);\n  priority_state_manager.initializePriorityFor(dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_);\n  {\n    absl::ReaderMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n    for (const auto& host : host_map_) {\n      priority_state_manager.registerHostForPriority(host.second.logical_host_,\n                                                     dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_);\n    }\n  }\n  priority_state_manager.updateClusterPrioritySet(\n      0, std::move(priority_state_manager.priorityState()[0].first), hosts_added, hosts_removed,\n      absl::nullopt, absl::nullopt, absl::nullopt);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::onDnsHostRemove(const std::string& host) {\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_removed;\n  {\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n    const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\n    ASSERT(host_map_it != host_map_.end());\n    hosts_removed.emplace_back(host_map_it->second.logical_host_);\n    host_map_.erase(host);\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"removing dfproxy cluster host '{}'\", host);\n  }\n  updatePriorityState({}, hosts_removed);\n}\n\nUpstream::HostConstSharedPtr\nCluster::LoadBalancer::chooseHost(Upstream::LoadBalancerContext* context) {\n  if (!context) {\n    return nullptr;\n  }\n\n  const Router::StringAccessor* dynamic_host_filter_state = nullptr;\n  if (context->requestStreamInfo()) {\n    dynamic_host_filter_state =\n        context->requestStreamInfo()->filterState().getDataReadOnly<Router::StringAccessor>(\n            DynamicHostFilterStateKey);\n  }\n\n  absl::string_view raw_host;\n  if (dynamic_host_filter_state) {\n    raw_host = dynamic_host_filter_state->asString();\n  } else if (context->downstreamHeaders()) {\n    raw_host = context->downstreamHeaders()->getHostValue();\n  } else if (context->downstreamConnection()) {\n    raw_host = context->downstreamConnection()->requestedServerName();\n  }\n\n  // For host lookup, we need to make sure to match the host of any DNS cache\n  // insert. Two code points currently do DNS cache insert: the http DFP filter,\n  // which inserts for HTTP traffic, and sets port based on the cluster's\n  // security level, and the SNI DFP network filter which sets port based on\n  // stream metadata, or configuration (which is then added as stream metadata).\n  const bool is_secure = cluster_.info()\n                             ->transportSocketMatcher()\n                             .resolve(nullptr)\n                             .factory_.implementsSecureTransport();\n  uint32_t port = is_secure ? 443 : 80;\n  if (context->requestStreamInfo()) {\n    const StreamInfo::UInt32Accessor* dynamic_port_filter_state =\n        context->requestStreamInfo()->filterState().getDataReadOnly<StreamInfo::UInt32Accessor>(\n            DynamicPortFilterStateKey);\n    if (dynamic_port_filter_state != nullptr && dynamic_port_filter_state->value() > 0 &&\n        dynamic_port_filter_state->value() <= 65535) {\n      port = dynamic_port_filter_state->value();\n    }\n  }\n\n  std::string host = Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfo::normalizeHostForDfp(raw_host, port);\n\n  if (host.empty()) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"host empty\");\n    return nullptr;\n  }\n\n  if (cluster_.enableSubCluster()) {\n    return cluster_.chooseHost(host, context);\n  }\n\n  {\n    absl::ReaderMutexLock lock{&cluster_.host_map_lock_};\n    const auto host_it = cluster_.host_map_.find(host);\n    if (host_it == cluster_.host_map_.end()) {\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"host {} not found\", host);\n      return nullptr;\n    } else {\n      if (host_it->second.logical_host_->coarseHealth() == Upstream::Host::Health::Unhealthy) {\n        ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"host {} is unhealthy\", host);\n        return nullptr;\n      }\n      host_it->second.shared_host_info_->touch();\n      return host_it->second.logical_host_;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nabsl::optional<Upstream::SelectedPoolAndConnection>\nCluster::LoadBalancer::selectExistingConnection(Upstream::LoadBalancerContext* /*context*/,\n                                                const Upstream::Host& host,\n                                                std::vector<uint8_t>& hash_key) {\n  const std::string& hostname = host.hostname();\n  if (hostname.empty()) {\n    return absl::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  LookupKey key = {hash_key, *host.address()};\n  auto it = connection_info_map_.find(key);\n  if (it == connection_info_map_.end()) {\n    return absl::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  for (auto& info : it->second) {\n    Envoy::Ssl::ConnectionInfoConstSharedPtr ssl = info.connection_->ssl();\n    ASSERT(ssl);\n    for (const std::string& san : ssl->dnsSansPeerCertificate()) {\n      if (Extensions::TransportSockets::Tls::Utility::dnsNameMatch(hostname, san)) {\n        return Upstream::SelectedPoolAndConnection{*info.pool_, *info.connection_};\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  return absl::nullopt;\n}\n\nOptRef<Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::ConnectionLifetimeCallbacks>\nCluster::LoadBalancer::lifetimeCallbacks() {\n  if (!cluster_.allowCoalescedConnections()) {\n    return {};\n  }\n  return makeOptRef<Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::ConnectionLifetimeCallbacks>(*this);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::LoadBalancer::onConnectionOpen(Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::Instance& pool,\n                                             std::vector<uint8_t>& hash_key,\n                                             const Network::Connection& connection) {\n  // Only coalesce connections that are over TLS.\n  if (!connection.ssl()) {\n    return;\n  }\n  const std::string alpn = connection.nextProtocol();\n  if (alpn != Http::Utility::AlpnNames::get().Http2 &&\n      alpn != Http::Utility::AlpnNames::get().Http3) {\n    // Only coalesce connections for HTTP/2 and HTTP/3.\n    return;\n  }\n  const LookupKey key = {hash_key, *connection.connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()};\n  ConnectionInfo info = {&pool, &connection};\n  connection_info_map_[key].push_back(info);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::LoadBalancer::onConnectionDraining(Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::Instance& pool,\n                                                 std::vector<uint8_t>& hash_key,\n                                                 const Network::Connection& connection) {\n  const LookupKey key = {hash_key, *connection.connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()};\n  connection_info_map_[key].erase(\n      std::remove_if(connection_info_map_[key].begin(), connection_info_map_[key].end(),\n                     [&pool, &connection](const ConnectionInfo& info) {\n                       return (info.pool_ == &pool && info.connection_ == &connection);\n                     }),\n      connection_info_map_[key].end());\n}\n\nabsl::StatusOr<std::pair<Upstream::ClusterImplBaseSharedPtr, Upstream::ThreadAwareLoadBalancerPtr>>\nClusterFactory::createClusterWithConfig(\n    const envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster& cluster,\n    const envoy::extensions::clusters::dynamic_forward_proxy::v3::ClusterConfig& proto_config,\n    Upstream::ClusterFactoryContext& context) {\n\n  Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCacheManagerFactoryImpl cache_manager_factory(\n      context.serverFactoryContext(), context.messageValidationVisitor());\n\n  envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster cluster_config = cluster;\n  if (!cluster_config.has_upstream_http_protocol_options()) {\n    // This sets defaults which will only apply if using old style http config.\n    // They will be a no-op if typed_extension_protocol_options are used for\n    // http config.\n    cluster_config.mutable_upstream_http_protocol_options()->set_auto_sni(true);\n    cluster_config.mutable_upstream_http_protocol_options()->set_auto_san_validation(true);\n  }\n\n  Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCacheManagerSharedPtr cache_manager =\n      cache_manager_factory.get();\n  auto dns_cache_or_error = cache_manager->getCache(proto_config.dns_cache_config());\n  RETURN_IF_STATUS_NOT_OK(dns_cache_or_error);\n\n  auto new_cluster =\n      std::shared_ptr<Cluster>(new Cluster(cluster_config, std::move(dns_cache_or_error.value()),\n                                           proto_config, context, std::move(cache_manager)));\n\n  Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DFPClusterStoreFactory cluster_store_factory(\n      context.serverFactoryContext().singletonManager());\n  cluster_store_factory.get()->save(new_cluster->info()->name(), new_cluster);\n\n  auto& options = new_cluster->info()->upstreamHttpProtocolOptions();\n\n  if (!proto_config.allow_insecure_cluster_options()) {\n    if (!options.has_value() ||\n        (!options.value().auto_sni() || !options.value().auto_san_validation())) {\n      return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n          \"dynamic_forward_proxy cluster must have auto_sni and auto_san_validation true unless \"\n          \"allow_insecure_cluster_options is set.\");\n    }\n  }\n  if (proto_config.has_sub_clusters_config() &&\n      proto_config.sub_clusters_config().lb_policy() ==\n          envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster_LbPolicy::Cluster_LbPolicy_CLUSTER_PROVIDED) {\n    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n        \"unsupported lb_policy 'CLUSTER_PROVIDED' in sub_cluster_config\");\n  }\n\n  auto lb = std::make_unique<Cluster::ThreadAwareLoadBalancer>(*new_cluster);\n  return std::make_pair(new_cluster, std::move(lb));\n}\n\nREGISTER_FACTORY(ClusterFactory, Upstream::ClusterFactory);\n\n} // namespace DynamicForwardProxy\n} // namespace Clusters\n} // namespace Extensions\n} // namespace Envoy\n",
  "path": "/workspace/source/extensions/clusters/dynamic_forward_proxy/cluster.cc",
  "security_risk": "LOW"

SizeIs(0))).Times(existing_hosts.size());\n    }\n    cluster_->initialize([] {});\n  }\n\n  Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCacheManagerSharedPtr get() override {\n    return dns_cache_manager_;\n  }\n\n  void makeTestHost(const std::string& host, const std::string& address) {\n    EXPECT_TRUE(host_map_.find(host) == host_map_.end());\n    host_map_[host] = strict.\n    EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], address()).Times(AtLeast(0));\n    EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], addressList()).Times(AtLeast(0));\n    EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], lb_factory_->create(lb_params_); }\n\n  Upstream::MockLoadBalancerContext* setHostAndReturnContext(const std::string& host) {\n    downstream_headers_.remove(\":authority\");\n    downstream_headers_.addCopy(\":authority\", host);\n    return &lb_context_;\n  }\n\n  Upstream::MockLoadBalancerContext* setFilterStateHostAndReturnContext(const std::string& host) {\n    StreamInfo::FilterState& filter_state =\n        const_cast<StreamInfo::FilterState&>(lb_context_.requestStreamInfo()->filterState());\n\n    filter_state.setData(\n        \"envoy.upstream.dynamic_host\", std::make_shared<Router::StringAccessorImpl>(host),\n        StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable, StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Connection,\n        StreamInfo::StreamSharingMayImpactPooling::SharedWithUpstreamConnection);\n\n    return &lb_context_;\n  }\n\n  void setOutlierFailed(const std::string& host) {\n    for (auto& h : cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()) {\n      if (h->hostname() == host) {\n        h->healthFlagSet(Upstream::Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_OUTLIER_CHECK);\n        break;\n      }\n h->healthFlagClear(Upstream::Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_OUTLIER_CHECK);\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  MOCK_METHOD(void, onMemberUpdateCb,\n              (const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_added, const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_removed));\n\n  NiceMock<Server::Configuration::MockServerFactoryContext> server_context_;\n  Stats::TestUtil::TestStore& stats_store_ = server_context_.store_;\n  Api::ApiPtr api_{Api::createApiForTest(stats_store_)};\n  Ssl::MockContextManager ssl_context_manager_;\n\n std::shared_ptr<Cluster> cluster_;\n  Upstream::ThreadAwareLoadBalancerPtr thread_aware_lb_;\n  Upstream::LoadBalancerFactorySharedPtr lb_factory_;\n  Upstream::LoadBalancerPtr lb_;\n create() method but thread aware load balancer will not use it.\n  NiceMock<Upstream::MockPrioritySet> worker_priority_set_;\n  Upstream::LoadBalancerParams lb_params_{worker_priority_set_, {}};\n\n  const std::string sub_cluster_yaml_config_ = R\"EOF(\nname: name\nconnect_timeout: 0.25s\ncluster_type:\n  name: dynamic_forward_proxy\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\n    sub_clusters_config:\n      max_sub_clusters: 1024\n)EOF\";\n\n  const std::string default_yaml_config_ = R\"EOF(\nname: name\nconnect_timeout: 0.25s\ncluster_type:\n  name: dynamic_forward_proxy\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\n    dns_cache_config:\n      name: foo\n      dns_lookup_family: AUTO\n)EOF\";\n\n  const std::string coalesce_connection_config_ = R\"EOF(\nname: name\nconnect_timeout: 0.25s\ncluster_type:\n  name: dynamic_forward_proxy\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\n    allow_coalesced_connections: true\n    dns_cache_config:\n      name: foo\n      dns_lookup_family: AUTO\n)EOF\";\n};\n\n// createSubClusterConfig twice.\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, CreateSubClusterConfig) {\n  initialize(sub_cluster_yaml_config_, false);\n\n  const std::string cluster_name = \"fake_cluster_name\";\n  const std::string host = \"localhost\";\n  const int port = 80;\n  std::pair<bool, absl::optional<envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster>> sub_cluster_pair =\n      cluster_->createSubClusterConfig(cluster_name, host, port);\n  EXPECT_EQ(true, sub_cluster_pair.first);\n  EXPECT_EQ(true, sub_cluster_pair.second.has_value());\n\n  // create again, already exists\n  sub_cluster_pair = cluster_->createSubClusterConfig(cluster_name, host, port);\n  EXPECT_EQ(true, sub_cluster_pair.first);\n  EXPECT_EQ(false, sub_cluster_pair.second.has_value());\n}\n\n// Basic flow of the cluster including adding hosts and removing them.\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, BasicFlow) {\n  initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\n  makeTestHost(\"host1:0\", \"1.2.3.4\");\n  InSequence s;\n\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"\")));\n\n  // Verify no host LB cases.\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"foo\")));\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->peekAnotherHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"foo\")));\n\n  // LB will immediately resolve host1.\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_EQ(1UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()[0]->address()->asString());\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  // After changing the address, LB will immediately resolve the new address with a refresh.\n  updateTestHostAddress(\"host1:0\", \"2.3.4.5\");\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(1)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_EQ(1UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"2.3.4.5:0\",\n            cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()[0]->address()->asString());\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"2.3.4.5:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  // Remove the host, LB will immediately fail to find the host in the map.\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(0), SizeIs(1)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostRemove(\"host1:0\");\n  EXPECT_EQ(0UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\")));\n}\n\n// Outlier detection\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, OutlierDetection) {\n  initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\n  makeTestHost(\"host1:0\", \"1.2.3.4\");\n  makeTestHost(\"host2:0\", \"5.6.7.8\");\n  InSequence s;\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host2:0\", host_map_[\"host2:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host2:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"5.6.7.8:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host2:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  setOutlierFailed(\"host1:0\");\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\")));\n\n  clearOutlierFailed(\"host1:0\");\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n}\n\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, LoadBalancerContextHostFromFilterState) {\n  initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\n  makeTestHost(\"host1:80\", \"1.2.3.4\");\n  InSequence s;\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:80\", initialize(coalesce_connection_config_, false);\n\n  const std::string hostname = \"mail.example.org\";\n  Upstream::MockHost host;\n  EXPECT_CALL(host, hostname()).WillRepeatedly(testing::ReturnRef(hostname));\n  Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address =\n      Network::Utility::resolveUrl(\"tcp://10.0.0.3:50000\");\n  EXPECT_CALL(host, address()).WillRepeatedly(testing::Return(address));\n  std::vector<uint8_t> hash_key = {1, 2, 3};\n\n  Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::MockInstance pool;\n  Envoy::Network::MockConnection connection;\n  OptRef<Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::ConnectionLifetimeCallbacks> lifetime_callbacks =\n      lb_->lifetimeCallbacks();\n  ASSERT_TRUE(lifetime_callbacks.has_value());\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(connection, connectionInfoProvider()).Times(testing::AnyNumber());\n  EXPECT_CALL(connection, nextProtocol()).WillRepeatedly(Return(\"h2\"));\n  auto ssl_info = std::make_shared<Ssl::MockConnectionInfo>();\n  EXPECT_CALL(connection, ssl()).WillRepeatedly(Return(ssl_info));\n  lifetime_callbacks->onConnectionOpen(pool, hash_key, connection);\n\n  std::vector<std::string> dns_sans = {\"www.example.org\", \"mail.example.org\"};\n  EXPECT_CALL(*ssl_info, dnsSansPeerCertificate()).WillRepeatedly(Return(dns_sans));\n\n  absl::optional<Upstream::SelectedPoolAndConnection> selection =\n      lb_->selectExistingConnection(&lb_context_, host, hash_key);\n\n  ASSERT_TRUE(selection.has_value());\n  EXPECT_EQ(&pool, &selection->pool_);\n  EXPECT_EQ(&connection, &selection->connection_);\n}\n\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, LoadBalancer_SelectPoolDrained) {\n  initialize(coalesce_connection_config_, false);\n\n  const std::string hostname = \"mail.example.org\";\n  Upstream::MockHost host;\n  EXPECT_CALL(host, hostname()).WillRepeatedly(testing::ReturnRef(hostname));\n  Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address =\n      Network::Utility::resolveUrl(\"tcp://10.0.0.3:50000\");\n  EXPECT_CALL(host, address()).WillRepeatedly(testing::Return(address));\n  std::vector<uint8_t> hash_key = {1, 2, 3};\n\n  Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::MockInstance pool;\n  Envoy::Network::MockConnection connection;\n  OptRef<Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::ConnectionLifetimeCallbacks> lifetime_callbacks =\n      lb_->lifetimeCallbacks();\n  ASSERT_TRUE(lifetime_callbacks.has_value());\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(connection, connectionInfoProvider()).Times(testing::AnyNumber());\n  EXPECT_CALL(connection, nextProtocol()).WillRepeatedly(Return(\"h2\"));\n  auto ssl_info = std::make_shared<Ssl::MockConnectionInfo>();\n  EXPECT_CALL(connection, ssl()).WillRepeatedly(Return(ssl_info));\n  lifetime_callbacks->onConnectionOpen(pool, hash_key, connection);\n\n  // Drain the connection then no verify that no connection is subsequently selected.\n  lifetime_callbacks->onConnectionDraining(pool, hash_key, connection);\n\n  absl::optional<Upstream::SelectedPoolAndConnection> selection =\n      lb_->selectExistingConnection(&lb_context_, host, hash_key);\n\n  ASSERT_FALSE(selection.has_value());\n}\n\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, LoadBalancer_SelectPoolInvalidAlpn) {\n  initialize(coalesce_connection_config_, false);\n\n  const std::string hostname api_{Api::createApiForTest(stats_store_)};\n\n  NiceMock<Ssl::MockContextManager> ssl_context_manager_;\n  Upstream::ClusterSharedPtr cluster_;\n  Upstream::ThreadAwareLoadBalancerPtr thread_aware_lb_;\n};\n\nTEST_F(ClusterFactoryTest, InvalidUpstreamHttpProtocolOptions) {\n  const std::string yaml_config = TestEnvironment::substitute(R\"EOF(\nname: name\nconnect_timeout: 0.25s\ncluster_type:\n  name: dynamic_forward_proxy\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\n    dns_cache_config:\n auto_san_validation true unless \"\n      \"allow_insecure_cluster_options is set.\");\n}\n\nTEST_F(ClusterFactoryTest, InsecureUpstreamHttpProtocolOptions) {\n  const std::string yaml_config = TestEnvironment::substitute(R\"EOF(\nname: name\nconnect_timeout: 0.25s\ncluster_type:\n  name: dynamic_forward_proxy\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\n    allow_insecure_cluster_options: true\n    dns_cache_config:\n      name: foo\nupstream_http_protocol_options: {}\n)EOF\");\n\n  createCluster(yaml_config);\n}\n\nTEST_F(ClusterFactoryTest, InvalidSubprotocolOptions) {\n  const std::string yaml_config = R\"EOF(\nname: name\nconnect_timeout: 0.25s\ncluster_type:\n  name: dynamic_forward_proxy\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\n    sub_clusters_config:\n \"envoy.upstream.dynamic_host\";\n  auto* factory =\n      Registry::FactoryRegistry<StreamInfo::FilterState::ObjectFactory>::getFactory(name);\n  ASSERT_NE(nullptr, factory);\n  EXPECT_EQ(name, factory->name());\n  const std::string host = \"site.com\";\n  auto object = factory->createFromBytes(host);\n  ASSERT_NE(nullptr, object);\n  EXPECT_EQ(host, object->serializeAsString());\n}\n\nTEST(ObjectFactory, DynamicPort) {\n  const std::string name = \"envoy.upstream.dynamic_port\";\n  auto* factory =\n      Registry::FactoryRegistry<StreamInfo::FilterState::ObjectFactory>::getFactory(name);\n  ASSERT_NE(nullptr, factory);\n  EXPECT_EQ(name, factory->name());\n  const std::string port = \"8080\";\n  auto object = factory->createFromBytes(port);\n  ASSERT_NE(nullptr, object);\n  EXPECT_EQ(port, object->serializeAsString());\n  ASSERT_EQ(nullptr, factory->createFromBytes(\"blah\"));\n}\n\n} // namespace DynamicForwardProxy\n} // namespace Clusters\n} // namespace Extensions\n} // namespace Envoy\n",

\\n1134:     load_balancer_config_ = std::move(lb_pair->config);\\n1135:     load_balancer_factory_ = lb_pair->factory;\\n1136:     lb_type_ = LoadBalancerType::LoadBalancingPolicyConfig;\\n1137: legacy config because all legacy config is wrapped in load_balancer_config_\\n1146:     // except the original_dst_lb_config.\\n1147:     lb_subset_ = nullptr;\\n1148:     if (config.lb_policy() != envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster::CLUSTER_PROVIDED) {\\n1149:       lb_policy_config_ = nullptr;\\n1150:     }\\n1151:   } else {\\n1152:     switch (config.lb_policy()) {\\n1153:       PANIC_ON_PROTO_ENUM_SENTINEL_VALUES;\\n1154:     case envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster::ROUND_ROBIN:\\n1155:       lb_type_ = LoadBalancerType::RoundRobin;\\n1156:       break;\\n1157:     case envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster::LEAST_REQUEST:\\n1158:       lb_type_ = LoadBalancerType::LeastRequest;\\n1159:       break;\\n1160:     case envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster::RANDOM:\\n1161: policy {} cannot be combined with lb_subset_config\\\",\\n1173:                         envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster::LbPolicy_Name(config.lb_policy())));\\n1174:       }\\n1175: \\n1176:       lb_type_ = LoadBalancerType::ClusterProvided;\\n1177:       break;\\n1178:     case envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster::LOAD_BALANCING_POLICY_CONFIG: {\\n1179:       // 'LOAD_BALANCING_POLICY_CONFIG' }\\n1305: \\n1306: // Configures the load balancer based on config.load_balancing_policy\\n1307: void ClusterInfoImpl::configureLbPolicies(const envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster& config,\\n1308:                                           Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& context) {\\n1309:   // Check if load_balancing_policy is set first.\\n1310:   if (!config.has_load_balancing_policy()) {\\n1311:     throwEnvoyExceptionOrPanic(\\\"cluster: field load_balancing_policy need to be set\\\");\\n1312:   }\\n1313: \\n1314:   if (config.has_lb_subset_config()) {\\n1315:     throwEnvoyExceptionOrPanic(\\n1316:         \\\"cluster: load_balancing_policy cannot be combined with lb_subset_config\\\");\\n1317:   }\\n1318: \\n1319:   if (config.has_common_lb_config()) {\\n1320:     const auto& lb_config = config.common_lb_config();\\n1321:     if (lb_config.has_zone_aware_lb_config() || lb_config.has_locality_weighted_lb_config() ||\\n1322:         lb_config.has_consistent_hashing_lb_config()) {\\n1323:       throwEnvoyExceptionOrPanic(\\n1324:           \\\"cluster: load_balancing_policy cannot be combined with partial fields \\\"\\n1325:           \\\"(zone_aware_lb_config, \\\"\\n1326:           \\\"locality_weighted_lb_config, consistent_hashing_lb_config) of common_lb_config\\\");\\n1327:     }\\n1328:   }\\n1329: \\n1330:   absl::InlinedVector<absl::string_view, 4> missing_policies;\\n1331:   for (const auto& policy : config.load_balancing_policy().policies()) {\\n1332:     TypedLoadBalancerFactory* factory =\\n1333:         Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<TypedLoadBalancerFactory>(\\n1334:             policy.typed_extension_config(), /*is_optional=*/true);\\n1335:     if (factory != nullptr) {\\n1336:       // Load and validate the configuration.\\n1337:       auto proto_message = factory->createEmptyConfigProto();\\n1338:       Config::Utility::translateOpaqueConfig(policy.typed_extension_config().typed_config(),\\n1339:                                              context.messageValidationVisitor(), *proto_message);\\n1340: \\n1341:       load_balancer_config_ =\\n1342:           factory->loadConfig(*proto_message, context.messageValidationVisitor());\\n1343: \\n1344:       load_balancer_factory_ = factory;\\n1345:       break;\\n1346:     }\\n1347:     missing_policies.push_back(policy.typed_extension_config().name());\\n1348:   }\\n1349: \\n1350:   if (load_balancer_factory_ == nullptr) {\\n1351:     throwEnvoyExceptionOrPanic(\\n1352:         fmt::format(\\\"cluster: didn't find a registered load balancer factory \\\"\\n1353: LoadBalancerType::LoadBalancingPolicyConfig;\\n1358: }\\n1359: \\n1360: ProtocolOptionsConfigConstSharedPtr\\n1361: ClusterInfoImpl::extensionProtocolOptions(const std::string& name) const {\\n1362:   auto i = extension_protocol_options_.find(name);\\n1363:   if (i != extension_protocol_options_.end()) {\\n1364:     return i->second;\\n1365:   }\\n1366:   return nullptr;\\n1367: }\\n1368: \\n1369: Network::UpstreamTransportSocketFactoryPtr createTransportSocketFactory(\\n1370:     const envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster& config,\\n1371:     Server::Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContext& factory_context) {\\n1372:   // If the cluster config doesn't have a transport socket configured, override with the default\\n1373:   // transport socket implementation based on the tls_context. We copy by value first then\\n1374:   // override if necessary.\\n1375:   auto transport_socket = config.transport_socket();\\n1376:   if (!config.has_transport_socket()) {\\n1377:     envoy::extensions::transport_sockets::raw_buffer::v3::RawBuffer raw_buffer;\\n1378:     transport_socket.mutable_typed_config()->PackFrom(raw_buffer);\\n1379: info_->endpointStats().membership_total_.set(hosts);\\n1532:         info_->endpointStats().membership_healthy_.set(healthy_hosts);\\n1533: \\n1552: std::tuple<HealthyHostVectorConstSharedPtr, DegradedHostVectorConstSharedPtr,\\n1553:            ExcludedHostVectorConstSharedPtr>\\n1554: ClusterImplBase::partitionHostList(const HostVector& hosts) {\\n1555:   auto healthy_list = std::make_shared<HealthyHostVector>();\\n1556:   auto degraded_list = std::make_shared<DegradedHostVector>();\\n1557:   auto excluded_list = std::make_shared<ExcludedHostVector>();\\n1558: \\n1559:   for (const auto& host : hosts) {\\n1560:     const Host::Health health_status = host->coarseHealth();\\n1561:     if (health_status == Host::Health::Healthy) {\\n1562:       healthy_list->get().emplace_back(host);\\n1563:     } else if (health_status == Host::Health::Degraded) {\\n1564:       degraded_list->get().emplace_back(host);\\n1565:     }\\n1566:     if (excludeBasedOnHealthFlag(*host)) {\\n1567:       excluded_list->get().emplace_back(host);\\n1568:     }\\n1569:   }\\n1570: \\n1571:   return std::make_tuple(healthy_list, degraded_list, excluded_list);\\n1572: }\\n1573: \\n1574: std::tuple<HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr, HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr,\\n1575: \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"1700:   ASSERT(!health_checker_);\\n1701:   health_checker_ = health_checker;\\n1702:   health_checker_->start();\\n1703:   health_checker_->addHostCheckCompleteCb(\\n1704:       [this](const HostSharedPtr& host, HealthTransition changed_state) -> void {\\n1705:         // If we get a health check completion that resulted in a state change, signal to\\n1706:         // update the host sets on all threads.\\n1707:         if (changed_state == HealthTransition::Changed) {\\n1708:           reloadHealthyHosts(host);\\n1709:         }\\n1710:       });\\n1711: }\\n1712: \\n1713: void ClusterImplBase::setOutlierDetector(const Outlier::DetectorSharedPtr& outlier_detector) {\\n1714:   if (!outlier_detector) {\\n1715:     return;\\n1716:   }\\n1717: \\n1718:   outlier_detector_ = outlier_detector;\\n1719:   outlier_detector_->addChangedStateCb(\\n1720:       [this](const HostSharedPtr& host) -> void { reloadHealthyHosts(host); });\\n1721: }\\n1722: \\n1723: void ClusterImplBase::reloadHealthyHosts(const HostSharedPtr& host) {\\n1724:   // Every time a host changes Health Check state we cause a full healthy host recalculation which\\n1725:   // for expensive LBs (ring, subset, etc.) can be quite time consuming. During startup, this\\n1726:   // can also block worker threads by doing this repeatedly. There is no reason to do this\\n1727:   // as we will not start taking traffic until we are initialized. By blocking Health Check\\n1728:   // updates while initializing we can avoid this.\\n1729:   if (initialization_complete_callback_ != nullptr) {\\n1730:     return;\\n1731:   }\\n1732: \\n1733:   reloadHealthyHostsHelper(host);\\n1734: }\\n1735: \\n1736: void ClusterImplBase::reloadHealthyHostsHelper(const HostSharedPtr&) {\\n1737:   const auto& host_sets = prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority();\\n1738:   for (size_t priority = 0; priority < host_sets.size(); ++priority) {\\n1739:     const auto& host_set = host_sets[priority];\\n1740:     // TODO(htuch): Can we skip these copies by exporting out const shared_ptr from HostSet?\\n1741:     HostVectorConstSharedPtr hosts_copy = std::make_shared<HostVector>(host_set->hosts());\\n1742: \\n1743:     HostsPerLocalityConstSharedPtr hosts_per_locality_copy = host_set->hostsPerLocality().clone();\\n1744:     prioritySet().updateHosts(priority,\\n1745:                               HostSetImpl::partitionHosts(hosts_copy, hosts_per_locality_copy),\\n1746:                               host_set->localityWeights(), {}, {}, absl::nullopt, absl::nullopt);\\n1747:   }\\n1748: }\\n1749: \\n1750: const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr\\n1751: ClusterImplBase::resolveProtoAddress(const envoy::config::core::v3::Address& address) {\\n1752:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\\n1753:     auto address_or_error = Network::Address::resolveProtoAddress(address);\\n1754:     THROW_IF_STATUS_NOT_OK(address_or_error, throw);\\n1755:     return address_or_error.value();\\n1756:   }\\n1757:   END_TRY\\n1758:   CATCH(EnvoyException & e, {\\n1759:     if (info_->type() == envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster::STATIC ||\\n1760:         info_->type() == envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster::EDS) {\\n1761:       throwEnvoyExceptionOrPanic(\\n1762:           fmt::format(\\\"{}. Consider setting resolver_name or setting cluster type \\\"\\n1763:                       \\\"to 'STRICT_DNS' or 'LOGICAL_DNS'\\\",\\n1764:                       e.what()));\\n1765:     }\\n1766:     throw e;\\n1767:   });\\n1768: }\\n1769: \\n1770: void ClusterImplBase::validateEndpointsForZoneAwareRouting(\\n1771:     const envoy::config::endpoint::v3::LocalityLbEndpoints& endpoints) const {\\n1772:   if (local_cluster_ && endpoints.priority() > 0) {\\n1773:     throwEnvoyExceptionOrPanic(\\n1774:         fmt::format(\\\"Unexpected non-zero priority for local cluster '{}'.\\\", info()->name()));\\n1775:   }\\n1776: }\\n1777: \\n1778: ClusterInfoImpl::OptionalClusterStats::OptionalClusterStats(\\n1779:     const envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster& config, Stats::Scope& stats_scope,\\n1780:     const ClusterManager& manager)\\n1781:     : timeout_budget_stats_(\\n1782:           (config.track_cluster_stats().timeout_budgets() || config.track_timeout_budgets())\\n1783:               ? std::make_unique<ClusterTimeoutBudgetStats>(generateTimeoutBudgetStats(\\n1784:                     stats_scope, manager.clusterTimeoutBudgetStatNames()))\\n1785:               : nullptr),\\n1786:       request_response_size_stats_(\\n1787:           (config.track_cluster_stats().request_response_sizes()\\n1788:                ? std::make_unique<ClusterRequestResponseSizeStats>(generateRequestResponseSizeStats(\\n1789:                      stats_scope, manager.clusterRequestResponseSizeStatNames()))\\n1790: envoy::config::core::v3::DEFAULT);\\n1799:   managers_[enumToInt(ResourcePriority::High)] =\\n1800:       load(config, runtime, cluster_name, stats_scope, envoy::config::core::v3::HIGH);\\n1801: }\\n1802: \\n1803: ClusterCircuitBreakersStats\\n1804: ClusterInfoImpl::generateCircuitBreakersStats(Stats::Scope& scope, Stats::StatName prefix,\\n1805:                                               bool track_remaining,\\n1806:                                               const ClusterCircuitBreakersStatNames& stat_names) {\\n1807:   auto make_gauge = [&stat_names, &scope, current_priority_hosts.size());\\\\n2140:   // Keep track of hosts we're adding (or replacing)\\\\n2141:   absl::flat_hash_set\\\\u003cstd::string\\\\u003e "{\"meta\": null, \"content\": [{\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"{\\\"content\\\":\\\"2150:     // To match a new host with an existing host means comparing their addresses.\\\\n2151:     auto existing_host = all_hosts.find(addressToString(host-\\\\u003eaddress()));\\\\n2152:     const bool existing_host_found = existing_host != all_hosts.end();\\\\n2153: \\\\n2154:     // Clear any pending deletion flag on an existing host in case it came back while it was\\\\n2155:     // being stabilized. We will set it again below if needed.\\\\n2156:     if (existing_host_found) {\\\\n2157:       existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003ehealthFlagClear(Host::HealthFlag::PENDING_DYNAMIC_REMOVAL);\\\\n2158:     }\\\\n2159: \\\\n2160:     // Check if in-place host update should be skipped, i.e. when the following criteria are met\\\\n2161:     // (currently there is only one criterion, but we might add more in the future):\\\\n2162:     // - The cluster health checker is activated and a new host is matched with the existing one,\\\\n2163:     //   but the health check address is different.\\\\n2164:     const bool health_check_address_changed =\\\\n2165:         (health_checker_ != nullptr \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 existing_host_found \\\\u0026\\\\u0026\\\\n2166:          *existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003ehealthCheckAddress() != *host-\\\\u003ehealthCheckAddress());\\\\n2167:     bool locality_changed = false;\\\\n2168:     locality_changed = (existing_host_found \\\\u0026\\\\u0026\\\\n2169:                         (!LocalityEqualTo()(host-\\\\u003elocality(), existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003elocality())));\\\\n2170:     if (locality_changed) {\\\\n2171:       hosts_with_updated_locality_for_current_priority.emplace(existing_host-\\\\u003efirst);\\\\n2172:     }\\\\n2173: \\\\n2174:     const bool active_health_check_flag_changed =\\\\n2175:         (health_checker_ != nullptr \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 existing_host_found \\\\u0026\\\\u0026\\\\n2176:          existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003edisableActiveHealthCheck() != host-\\\\u003edisableActiveHealthCheck());\\\\n2177:     if (active_health_check_flag_changed) {\\\\n2178:       hosts_with_active_health_check_flag_changed.emplace(existing_host-\\\\u003efirst);\\\\n2179:     }\\\\n2180:     const bool skip_inplace_host_update =\\\\n2181:         health_check_address_changed || locality_changed || active_health_check_flag_changed;\\\\n2182: \\\\n2183:     // When there is a match and we decided to do in-place update, we potentially update the\\\\n2184:     // host's health check flag and metadata. Afterwards, the host is pushed back into the\\\\n2185:     // final_hosts, i.e. hosts that should be preserved in the current priority.\\\\n2186:     if (existing_host_found \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !skip_inplace_host_update) {\\\\n2187:       existing_hosts_for_current_priority.emplace(existing_host-\\\\u003efirst);\\\\n2188:       // If we find a host matched based on address, we keep it. However we do change weight\\\\n2189:       // inline so do that here.\\\\n2190:       if (host-\\\\u003eweight() \\\\u003e max_host_weight) {\\\\n2191:         max_host_weight = host-\\\\u003eweight();\\\\n2192:       }\\\\n2193:       if (existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003eweight() != host-\\\\u003eweight()) {\\\\n2194:         existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003eweight(host-\\\\u003eweight());\\\\n2195:         // We do full host set rebuilds so that load balancers can do pre-computation of data\\\\n2196:         // structures based on host weight. This may become a performance problem in certain\\\\n2197:         // deployments so it is runtime feature guarded and may also need to be configurable\\\\n2198:         // and/or dynamic in the future.\\\\n2199:         hosts_changed = true;\\\\n2200:       }\\\\n2201: \\\\n2202:       hosts_changed |= updateEdsHealthFlag(*host, *existing_host-\\\\u003esecond);\\\\n2203: \\\\n2204:       // Did metadata change?\\\\n2205:       bool metadata_changed = true;\\\\n2206:       if (host-\\\\u003emetadata() \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003emetadata()) {\\\\n2207:         metadata_changed = !Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer::Equivalent(\\\\n2208:             *host-\\\\u003emetadata(), *existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003emetadata());\\\\n2209:       } else if (!host-\\\\u003emetadata() \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003emetadata()) {\\\\n2210:         metadata_changed = false;\\\\n2211:       }\\\\n2212: \\\\n2213:       if (metadata_changed) {\\\\n2214:         // First, update the entire metadata for the endpoint.\\\\n2215:         existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003emetadata(host-\\\\u003emetadata());\\\\n2216: \\\\n2217:         // Also, given that the canary attribute of an endpoint is derived from its metadata\\\\n2218:         // (e.g.: from envoy.lb/canary), we do a blind update here since it's cheaper than testing\\\\n2219:         // to see if it actually changed. We must update this besides just updating the metadata,\\\\n2220:         // because it'll be used by the router filter to compute upstream stats.\\\\n2221:         existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003ecanary(host-\\\\u003ecanary());\\\\n2222: \\\\n2223:         // If metadata changed, we need to rebuild. See github issue #3810.\\\\n2224:         hosts_changed = true;\\\\n2225:       }\\\\n2226: \\\\n2227:       // Did the priority change?\\\\n2228:       if (host-\\\\u003epriority() != existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003epriority()) {\\\\n2229:         existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003epriority(host-\\\\u003epriority());\\\\n2230:         hosts_added_to_current_priority.emplace_back(existing_host-\\\\u003esecond);\\\\n2231:       }\\\\n2232: \\\\n2233:       final_hosts.push_back(existing_host-\\\\u003esecond);\\\\n2234:     } else {\\\\n2235:       new_hosts_for_current_priority.emplace(addressToString(host-\\\\u003eaddress()));\\\\n2236:       if (host-\\\\u003eweight() \\\\u003e max_host_weight) {\\\\n2237: host with the same health checker, the\\\\n2242:       // active health-status is kept.\\\\n2243:       if (health_checker_ != nullptr \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !host-\\\\u003edisableActiveHealthCheck()) {\\\\n2244:         if (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\\\\n2245:                 \\\\\\\"envoy.reloadable_features.keep_endpoint_active_hc_status_on_locality_update\\\\\\\")) {\\\\n2246:           if (existing_host_found \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !health_check_address_changed \\\\u0026\\\\u0026\\\\n2247:               !active_health_check_flag_changed) {\\\\n2248:             // If there's an existing host, use the same active health-status.\\\\n2249:             // The existing host can be marked PENDING_ACTIVE_HC or\\\\n2250:             // ACTIVE_HC_TIMEOUT if it is also marked with FAILED_ACTIVE_HC.\\\\n2251:             ASSERT(!existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003ehealthFlagGet(Host::HealthFlag::PENDING_ACTIVE_HC) ||\\\\n2252:                    existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003ehealthFlagGet(Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_ACTIVE_HC));\\\\n2253:             ASSERT(!existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003ehealthFlagGet(Host::HealthFlag::ACTIVE_HC_TIMEOUT) ||\\\\n2254: |\\\\n2260:                 enumToInt(Host::HealthFlag::ACTIVE_HC_TIMEOUT);\\\\n2261: \\\\n2262:             const uint32_t existing_host_statuses = existing_host-\\\\u003esecond-\\\\u003ehealthFlagsGetAll();\\\\n2263:             host-\\\\u003ehealthFlagsSetAll(existing_host_statuses \\\\u0026 active_hc_statuses_mask);\\\\n2264:           } else {\\\\n2265:             // No previous known host, mark it as failed active HC.\\\\n2266:             host-\\\\u003ehealthFlagSet(Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_ACTIVE_HC);\\\\n2267: \\\\n2268:             // If we want to exclude hosts until they have been health checked, mark them with\\\\n2269:             // a flag to indicate that they have not been health checked yet.\\\\n2270:             if (info_-\\\\u003ewarmHosts()) {\\\\n2271:               host-\\\\u003ehealthFlagSet(Host::HealthFlag::PENDING_ACTIVE_HC);\\\\n2272:             }\\\\n2273:           }\\\\n2274:         } else {\\\\n2275:           host-\\\\u003ehealthFlagSet(Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_ACTIVE_HC);\\\\n2276: \\\\n2277:           // If we want to exclude hosts until they have been health checked, mark them with\\\\n2278:           // a flag to indicate that they have not been health checked yet.\\\\n2279:           if (info_-\\\\u003ewarmHosts()) {\\\\n2280:             host-\\\\u003ehealthFlagSet(Host::HealthFlag::PENDING_ACTIVE_HC);\\\\n2281:           }\\\\n2282:         }\\\\n2283:       }\\\\n2284: \\\\n2285:       final_hosts.push_back(host);\\\\n2286:       hosts_added_to_current_priority.push_back(host);\\\\n2287:     }\\\\n2288:   }\\\\n2289: \\\\n2290:   // Remove hosts from current_priority_hosts that were matched to an existing host in the\\\\n2291:   // previous loop.\\\\n2292:   auto erase_from =\\\\n2293:       std::remove_if(current_priority_hosts.begin(), current_priority_hosts.end(),\\\\n2294: {\\\\n2299:                          existing_hosts_for_current_priority.erase(existing_itr);\\\\n2300:                          return true;\\\\n2301:                        }\\\\n2302: \\\\n2303: hosts during this iteration from a different priority, then we've moved\\\\n2308:   // a host from another priority into this one, so we should mark the priority as having changed.\\\\n2309:   if (!existing_hosts_for_current_priority.empty()) {\\\\n2310:     hosts_changed = true;\\\\n2311:   }\\\\n2312: \\\\n2313:   // The remaining hosts are hosts that are not referenced in the config update. We remove them\\\\n2314:   // from the priority if any of the following is true:\\\\n2315:   // - Active health checking is not enabled.\\\\n2316:   // - The removed hosts are failing active health checking OR have been explicitly marked as\\\\n2317:   //   unhealthy by a previous EDS update. We do not count outlier as a reason to remove a host\\\\n2318:   //   or any other future health condition that may be added so we do not use the coarseHealth()\\\\n2319:   //   API.\\\\n2320:   // - We have explicitly configured the cluster to remove hosts regardless of active health\\\\n2321:   // status.\\\\n2322:   const bool dont_remove_healthy_hosts =\\\\n2323:       health_checker_ != nullptr \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !info()-\\\\u003edrainConnectionsOnHostRemoval();\\\\n2324:   if (!current_priority_hosts.empty() \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 dont_remove_healthy_hosts) {\\\\n2325:     erase_from = std::remove_if(\\\\n2326:         current_priority_hosts.begin(), current_priority_hosts.end(),\\\\n2327:         [\\\\u0026all_new_hosts, \\\\u0026new_hosts_for_current_priority,\\\\n2328:          \\\\u0026hosts_with_updated_locality_for_current_priority,\\\\n2329:          \\\\u0026hosts_with_active_health_check_flag_changed, \\\\u0026final_hosts,\\\\n2330:          \\\\u0026max_host_weight](const HostSharedPtr\\\\u0026 p) {\\\\n2331:           // This host has already been added as a new host in the\\\\n2332:           // new_hosts_for_current_priority. Return false here to make sure that host\\\\n2333:           // reference with older locality gets cleaned up from the priority.\\\\n2334:           if (hosts_with_updated_locality_for_current_priority.contains(p-\\\\u003eaddress()-\\\\u003easString())) {\\\\n2335:             return false;\\\\n2336:           }\\\\n2337: \\\\n2338:           if (hosts_with_active_health_check_flag_changed.contains(p-\\\\u003eaddress()-\\\\u003easString())) {\\\\n2339:             return false;\\\\n2340:           }\\\\n2341: \\\\n2342:           if (all_new_hosts.contains(p-\\\\u003eaddress()-\\\\u003easString()) \\\\u0026\\\\u0026\\\\n2343:               !new_hosts_for_current_priority.contains(p-\\\\u003eaddress()-\\\\u003easString())) {\\\\n2344:             // If the address is being completely deleted from this priority, but is\\\\n2345:             // referenced from another priority, then we assume that the other\\\\n2346:             // priority will perform an in-place update to re-use the existing Host.\\\\n2347:             // We should therefore not mark it as PENDING_DYNAMIC_REMOVAL, but\\\\n2348:             // instead remove it immediately from this priority.\\\\n2349:             // Example: health check address changed and priority also changed\\\\n2350:             return false;\\\\n2351: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--5160151e@9d4d5e2281b6fa047a07fc76c2b33a88327e354f/-/blob/source/common/upstream/upstream_impl.cc%3FL2150-2350\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"2150:     // To match a new host with an existing host means comparing their addresses.\\n2151:     auto existing_host = all_hosts.find(addressToString(host->address()));\\n2152:     const bool existing_host_found = existing_host != all_hosts.end();\\n2153: \\n2154:     // Clear any pending deletion flag on an existing host in case it came back while it was\\n2155:     // being stabilized. We will set it again below if needed.\\n2156:     if (existing_host_found) {\\n2157:       existing_host->second->healthFlagClear(Host::HealthFlag::PENDING_DYNAMIC_REMOVAL);\\n2158:     }\\n2159: \\n2160:     // Check if in-place host update should be skipped, i.e. when the following criteria are met\\n2161:     // (currently there is only one criterion, but we might add more in the future):\\n2162:     // - The cluster health checker is activated and a new host is matched with the existing one,\\n2163:     //   but the health check address is different.\\n2164:     const bool health_check_address_changed =\\n2165:         (health_checker_ != nullptr && existing_host_found &&\\n2166:          *existing_host->second->healthCheckAddress() != *host->healthCheckAddress());\\n2167:     bool locality_changed = false;\\n2168:     locality_changed = (existing_host_found &&\\n2169:                         (!LocalityEqualTo()(host->locality(), existing_host->second->locality())));\\n2170:     if (locality_changed) {\\n2171:       hosts_with_updated_locality_for_current_priority.emplace(existing_host->first);\\n2172:     }\\n2173: \\n2174:     const bool active_health_check_flag_changed =\\n2175:         (health_checker_ != nullptr && existing_host_found &&\\n2176:          existing_host->second->disableActiveHealthCheck() != host->disableActiveHealthCheck());\\n2177:     if (active_health_check_flag_changed) {\\n2178:       hosts_with_active_health_check_flag_changed.emplace(existing_host->first);\\n2179:     }\\n2180:     const bool skip_inplace_host_update =\\n2181:         health_check_address_changed || locality_changed || active_health_check_flag_changed;\\n2182: \\n2183:     // When there is a match and we decided to do in-place update, we potentially update the\\n2184:     // host's health check flag and metadata. Afterwards, the host is pushed back into the\\n2185:     // final_hosts, i.e. hosts that should be preserved in the current priority.\\n2186:     if (existing_host_found && !skip_inplace_host_update) {\\n2187:       existing_hosts_for_current_priority.emplace(existing_host->first);\\n2188:       // If we find a host matched based on address, we keep it. However we do change weight\\n2189:       // inline so do that here.\\n2190:       if (host->weight() > max_host_weight) {\\n2191:         max_host_weight = host->weight();\\n2192:       }\\n2193:       if (existing_host->second->weight() != host->weight()) {\\n2194:         existing_host->second->weight(host->weight());\\n2195:         // We do full host set rebuilds so that load balancers can do pre-computation of data\\n2196:         // structures based on host weight. This may become a performance problem in certain\\n2197:         // deployments so it is runtime feature guarded and may also need to be configurable\\n2198:         // and/or dynamic in the future.\\n2199:         hosts_changed = true;\\n2200:       }\\n2201: \\n2202:       hosts_changed |= updateEdsHealthFlag(*host, *existing_host->second);\\n2203: \\n2204:       // Did metadata change?\\n2205:       bool metadata_changed = true;\\n2206:       if (host->metadata() && existing_host->second->metadata()) {\\n2207:         metadata_changed = !Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer::Equivalent(\\n2208:             *host->metadata(), *existing_host->second->metadata());\\n2209:       } else if (!host->metadata() && !existing_host->second->metadata()) {\\n2210:         metadata_changed = false;\\n2211:       }\\n2212: \\n2213:       if (metadata_changed) {\\n2214:         // First, update the entire metadata for the endpoint.\\n2215:         existing_host->second->metadata(host->metadata());\\n2216: \\n2217:         // Also, given that the canary attribute of an endpoint is derived from its metadata\\n2218:         // (e.g.: from envoy.lb/canary), we do a blind update here since it's cheaper than testing\\n2219:         // to see if it actually changed. We must update this besides just updating the metadata,\\n2220:         // because it'll be used by the router filter to compute upstream stats.\\n2221:         existing_host->second->canary(host->canary());\\n2222: \\n2223:         // If metadata changed, we need to rebuild. See github issue #3810.\\n2224:         hosts_changed = true;\\n2225:       }\\n2226: \\n2227:       // Did the priority change?\\n2228:       if (host->priority() != existing_host->second->priority()) {\\n2229:         existing_host->second->priority(host->priority());\\n2230:         hosts_added_to_current_priority.emplace_back(existing_host->second);\\n2231:       }\\n2232: \\n2233:       final_hosts.push_back(existing_host->second);\\n2234:     } else {\\n2235:       new_hosts_for_current_priority.emplace(addressToString(host->address()));\\n2236:       if (host->weight() > max_host_weight) {\\n2237:         max_host_weight = host->weight();\\n2238:       }\\n2239: \\n2240:       // If we are depending on a health checker, we initialize to unhealthy.\\n2241:       // If there's an existing host with the same health checker, the\\n2242:       // active health-status is kept.\\n2243:       if (health_checker_ != nullptr && !host->disableActiveHealthCheck()) {\\n2244:         if (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\\n2245:                 \\\"envoy.reloadable_features.keep_endpoint_active_hc_status_on_locality_update\\\")) {\\n2246:           if (existing_host_found && !health_check_address_changed &&\\n2247:               !active_health_check_flag_changed) {\\n2248:             // If there's an existing host, use the same active health-status.\\n2249:             // The existing host can be marked PENDING_ACTIVE_HC or\\n2250:             // ACTIVE_HC_TIMEOUT if it is also marked with FAILED_ACTIVE_HC.\\n2251:             ASSERT(!existing_host->second->healthFlagGet(Host::HealthFlag::PENDING_ACTIVE_HC) ||\\n2252:                    existing_host->second->healthFlagGet(Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_ACTIVE_HC));\\n2253:             ASSERT(!existing_host->second->healthFlagGet(Host::HealthFlag::ACTIVE_HC_TIMEOUT) ||\\n2254:                    existing_host->second->healthFlagGet(Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_ACTIVE_HC));\\n2255: \\n2256:             constexpr uint32_t active_hc_statuses_mask =\\n2257:                 enumToInt(Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_ACTIVE_HC) |\\n2258:                 enumToInt(Host::HealthFlag::DEGRADED_ACTIVE_HC) |\\n2259:                 enumToInt(Host::HealthFlag::PENDING_ACTIVE_HC) |\\n2260:                 enumToInt(Host::HealthFlag::ACTIVE_HC_TIMEOUT);\\n2261: \\n2262:             const uint32_t existing_host_statuses = existing_host->second->healthFlagsGetAll();\\n2263:             host->healthFlagsSetAll(existing_host_statuses & active_hc_statuses_mask);\\n2264:           } else {\\n2265:             // No previous known host, mark it as failed active HC.\\n2266:             host->healthFlagSet(Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_ACTIVE_HC);\\n2267: \\n2268:             // If we want to exclude hosts until they have been health checked, mark them with\\n2269:             // a flag to indicate that they have not been health checked yet.\\n2270:             if (info_->warmHosts()) {\\n2271:               host->healthFlagSet(Host::HealthFlag::PENDING_ACTIVE_HC);\\n2272: have been health checked, mark them with\\n2278:           // a flag to indicate that they have not been health checked yet.\\n2279:           if (info_->warmHosts()) {\\n2280:             host->healthFlagSet(Host::HealthFlag::PENDING_ACTIVE_HC);\\n2281:           }\\n2282:         }\\n2283:       }\\n2284: \\n2285:       final_hosts.push_back(host);\\n2286:       hosts_added_to_current_priority.push_back(host);\\n2287:     }\\n2288:   }\\n2289: \\n2290:   // Remove hosts from current_priority_hosts that were matched to an existing host in the\\n2291:   // previous loop.\\n2292:   auto erase_from =\\n2293:       std::remove_if(current_priority_hosts.begin(), current_priority_hosts.end(),\\n2294:                      [&existing_hosts_for_current_priority](const HostSharedPtr& p) {\\n2295:                        auto existing_itr =\\n2296:                            existing_hosts_for_current_priority.find(p->address()->asString());\\n2297: \\n2298:                        if (existing_itr != existing_hosts_for_current_priority.end()) {\\n2299: false;\\n2304:                      });\\n2305:   current_priority_hosts.erase(erase_from, current_priority_hosts.end());\\n2306: \\n2307:   // If we saw existing hosts during this iteration from a different priority, then we've moved\\n2308:   // a host from another priority into this one, so we should mark the priority as having changed.\\n2309:   if (!existing_hosts_for_current_priority.empty()) {\\n2310:     hosts_changed = true;\\n2311:   }\\n2312: \\n2313:   // The remaining hosts are hosts that are not referenced in the config update. We remove them\\n2314:   // from the priority if any of the following is true:\\n2315:   // - Active health checking is not enabled.\\n2316:   // - The removed hosts are failing active health checking OR have been explicitly marked as\\n2317:   //   unhealthy by a previous EDS update. We do not count outlier as a reason to remove a host\\n2318:   //   or any other future health condition that may be added so we do not use the coarseHealth()\\n2319:   //   API.\\n2320:   // - We have explicitly configured the cluster to remove hosts regardless of active health\\n2321:   // status.\\n2322:   const bool dont_remove_healthy_hosts =\\n2323:       health_checker_ != nullptr && !info()->drainConnectionsOnHostRemoval();\\n2324:   if (!current_priority_hosts.empty() && dont_remove_healthy_hosts) {\\n2325:     erase_from = std::remove_if(\\n2326:         current_priority_hosts.begin(), current_priority_hosts.end(),\\n2327:         [&all_new_hosts, &new_hosts_for_current_priority,\\n2328:          &hosts_with_updated_locality_for_current_priority,\\n2329:          &hosts_with_active_health_check_flag_changed, &final_hosts,\\n2330:          &max_host_weight](const HostSharedPtr& p) {\\n2331:           // This host has already been added as a new host in the\\n2332:           // new_hosts_for_current_priority. Return false here to make sure that host\\n2333:           // reference with older locality gets cleaned up from the priority.\\n2334:           if (hosts_with_updated_locality_for_current_priority.contains(p->address()->asString())) {\\n2335:             return false;\\n2336:           }\\n2337: \\n2338:           if (hosts_with_active_health_check_flag_changed.contains(p->address()->asString())) {\\n2339:             return false;\\n2340:           }\\n2341: \\n2342:           if (all_new_hosts.contains(p->address()->asString()) &&\\n2343:               !new_hosts_for_current_priority.contains(p->address()->asString())) {\\n2344:             // If the address is being completely deleted from this priority, but is\\n2345:             // referenced from another priority, then we assume that the other\\n2346:             // priority will perform an in-place update to re-use the existing Host.\\n2347:             // We should therefore not mark it as PENDING_DYNAMIC_REMOVAL, but\\n2348:             // instead remove it immediately from this priority.\\n2349:             // Example: health check address changed and priority also changed\\n2350:             return false;\\n2351: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--5160151e@9d4d5e2281b6fa047a07fc76c2b33a88327e354f/-/blob/source/common/upstream/upstream_impl.cc%3FL2150-2350\"}, \"isError\": false}"

  "command": "cat > /tmp/patch_upstream.py << 'EOF'\n# This script creates the modified upstream_impl.cc\n# The only change is adding debug logging in updateCrossPriorityHostMap\n\ncontent = open('/tmp/upstream_impl_original.cc').read()\n\nold = '''  for (const auto& host : hosts_removed) {\n    mutable_cross_priority_host_map_->erase(addressToString(host->address()));\n  }\n\n  for (const auto& host : hosts_added) {\n    mutable_cross_priority_host_map_->insert({addressToString(host->address()), host});\n  }'''\n\nnew = '''  for (const auto& host : hosts_removed) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"removing host {} from cross-priority host map\",\n              addressToString(host->address()));\n    mutable_cross_priority_host_map_->erase(addressToString(host->address()));\n  }\n\n  for (const auto& host : hosts_added) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"adding host {} to cross-priority host map\",\n              addressToString(host->address()));\n    mutable_cross_priority_host_map_->insert({addressToString(host->address()), host});\n  }'''\n\nresult = content.replace(old, new)\nif result == content:\n    print(\"ERROR: No replacement made!\")\nelse:\n    print(\"Replacement successful!\")\n    open('/workspace/source/common/upstream/upstream_impl.cc', 'w').write(result)\nEOF\necho \"Script created\"",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

config.typed_extension_protocol_options()) {\\n123:     auto& name = it.first;\\n124:     auto object = createProtocolOptionsConfig(name, it.second, factory_context);\\n125:     if (object != nullptr) {\\n126:       options[name] = std::move(object);\\n127:     }\\n128:   }\\n129: \\n130:   return options;\\n131: }\\n132: \\n133: // Updates the EDS health flags for an existing host to match the new host.\\n134: // @param updated_host the new host to read health flag values from.\\n135: // @param existing_host the host to update.\\n136: // @return bool whether the flag update caused the host health to change.\\n137: bool updateEdsHealthFlag(const Host& updated_host, Host& existing_host) {\\n138:   auto updated_eds_health_status = updated_host.edsHealthStatus();\\n139: \\n140:   // Check if the health flag has changed.\\n141:   if (updated_eds_health_status == existing_host.edsHealthStatus()) {\\n142:     return false;\\n143:   }\\n144: \\n145:   const auto previous_health = existing_host.coarseHealth();\\n146:   existing_host.setEdsHealthStatus(updated_eds_health_status);\\n147: \\n148:   // Rebuild if changing the flag affected the host health.\\n149:   return previous_health != existing_host.coarseHealth();\\n150: }\\n151: \\n152: // Converts a set of hosts into a HostVector, excluding certain hosts.\\n153: // @param hosts hosts to convert\\n154: // @param excluded_hosts hosts to exclude from the resulting vector.\\n155: HostVector filterHosts(const absl::node_hash_set<HostSharedPtr>& hosts,\\n156:                        const absl::node_hash_set<HostSharedPtr>& excluded_hosts) {\\n157:   HostVector net_hosts;\\n158:   net_hosts.reserve(hosts.size());\\n159: \\n160:   for (const auto& host : hosts) {\\n161:     if (excluded_hosts.find(host) == excluded_hosts.end()) {\\n162:       net_hosts.emplace_back(host);\\n163:     }\\n164:   }\\n165: \\n166:   return net_hosts;\\n167: }\\n168: \\n169: Stats::ScopeSharedPtr generateStatsScope(const envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster& config,\\n170:                                          Stats::Store& stats) {\\n171:   return stats.createScope(fmt::format(\\n172:       \\\"cluster.{}.\\\", config.alt_stat_name().empty() ? config.name() : config.alt_stat_name()));\\n173: }\\n174: \\n175: Network::ConnectionSocket::OptionsSharedPtr\\n176: buildBaseSocketOptions(const envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster& cluster_config,\\n177:                        const envoy::config::core::v3::BindConfig& bootstrap_bind_config) {\\n178:   Network::ConnectionSocket::OptionsSharedPtr base_options =\\n179:       std::make_shared<Network::ConnectionSocket::Options>();\\n180: \\n181:   // The process-wide `signal()` handling may fail to handle SIGPIPE if overridden\\n182:   // in the process (i.e., on a mobile client). Some OSes support handling it at the socket layer:\\n183:   if (ENVOY_SOCKET_SO_NOSIGPIPE.hasValue()) {\\n184:     Network::Socket::appendOptions(base_options,\\n185:                                    Network::SocketOptionFactory::buildSocketNoSigpipeOptions());\\n186:   }\\n187:   // Cluster IP_FREEBIND settings, when set, will override the cluster manager wide settings.\\n188:   if ((bootstrap_bind_config.freebind().value() &&\\n189:        !cluster_config.upstream_bind_config().has_freebind()) ||\\n190:       cluster_config.upstream_bind_config().freebind().value()) {\\n191:     Network::Socket::appendOptions(base_options,\\n192:                                    Network::SocketOptionFactory::buildIpFreebindOptions());\\n193:   }\\n194:   if (cluster_config.upstream_connection_options().has_tcp_keepalive()) {\\n195:     Network::Socket::appendOptions(base_options,\\n196:                                    Network::SocketOptionFactory::buildTcpKeepaliveOptions(\\n197:                                        parseTcpKeepaliveConfig(cluster_config)));\\n198:   }\\n199: \\n200:   return base_options;\\n201: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--5160151e@9d4d5e2281b6fa047a07fc76c2b33a88327e354f/-/blob/source/common/upstream/upstream_impl.cc%3FL1-200\"}, \"isError\": false}"
